column1: That is most terrifying. I do not know how I might be able to help you.
column2: 這太可怕了。我不知道自己能幫上你什麼忙。

column1: That is very kind of you. Thank you for not using up more powder than was necessary.
column2: 你真是太好心了。謝謝你沒用掉多餘的粉末。

column1: That looks and smells delicious, Hero-friend. Shema and I will enjoy this treat. Thank you very much.
column2: 看著就香,英雄朋友。雪瑪和我會好好享用這份美味。非常感謝你。

column1: That must have been a frightful experience; being turned into a frog and held over a boiling cauldron.
column2: 那肯定是段駭人的經歷——被變成青蛙,還被吊在滾燙的大鍋上。

column1: That's all well and good, but I'd rather be conducting a little business, if there's any to be conducted.
column2: 這都很好,不過要是有生意可做,我倒寧願做點買賣。

column1: That's nice of you to show me your money.
column2: 你把錢秀給我看,真好心。

column1: That's not a good idea.
column2: 這主意不妙。

column1: That's not the most sanitary of places to stick your hand.
column2: 把手伸進那種地方,可不怎麼衛生。

column1: That's one thirsty Saurus.
column2: 這索魯斯可真渴。

column1: That was a great fight you put up! You've got what it takes to be amongst the best members of EOF.
column2: 你這一仗打得漂亮!你有躋身永恆戰士團頂尖成員的本錢。

column1: That was a waste.
column2: 真是浪費。

column1: That which I lost[is now returned,[I give you the praise[of gratitude earned.
column2: 我所失之物[如今已歸還,[我以應得的[感激來頌讚你。

column1: That won't work. The spell Ad Avis placed around the ritual chamber stops all magic from getting in.
column2: 那行不通。阿德·阿維斯在祭儀室四周下的咒,擋住了一切外來的魔法。

column1: That would be a heated conversation.
column2: 那會是場「火熱」的對話。

column1: That would give new definition to the term "lucky dip."
column2: 那會給「幸運抽抽樂」一詞下個全新的定義。

column1: That would guarantee a quick one-way trip to the dungeon.
column2: 那準保讓你直送地牢、有去無回。

column1: That would not be a very good idea. You prefer navigating the city without getting lost all the time.
column2: 那可不是什麼好主意。你還是寧願在城裡走動時別老是迷路。

column1: That wouldn't be a good idea right now.
column2: 現在這麼做不妙。

column1: That wouldn't be very heroic.
column2: 那可不太像英雄的作為。

column1: That wouldn't do you any good here.
column2: 在這兒那對你沒半點好處。

column1: That would probably leave you with a permanent case of halitosis.
column2: 那大概會讓你落下永久性口臭。

column1: The acrobat just frowns at you.
column2: 那雜耍藝人只是朝你皺眉。

column1: The Adventurer's Guild is on the west end of the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在戰士廣場的西端。

column1: The Astrologer has gone to bed for the night.
column2: 占星師已經就寢了。

column1: The barrel contains a pile of rotting fruit. Phew!
column2: 桶裡裝著一堆腐爛的水果。噁!

column1: The barrier seems to be the only thing around here that's in good shape. You can't budge it.
column2: 這障礙物似乎是這附近唯一完好的東西。你絲毫撼動不了它。

column1: The beast is much too large to fetch
column2: 這野獸太大了,「取物」術搬不動。

column1: The beast is much too large to fetch, and you can't control the spell well enough to get only some fur.
column2: 這野獸太大,「取物」術搬不動,你也沒法精準到只取一撮毛。

column1: The beast only reacts with a few quick blinks.
column2: 那野獸只是飛快地眨了幾下眼。

column1: The beast seems at peace for a moment, but not for long.
column2: 那野獸暫時平靜了一會兒,卻維持不久。

column1: The beggar accepts the food gratefully.
column2: 乞丐感激地接過了食物。

column1: The beggar accepts the slice of pizza gratefully.
column2: 乞丐感激地接過了那片披薩。

column1: The beggar gives you a strange look, but says nothing.
column2: 乞丐古怪地瞅了你一眼,卻什麼也沒說。

column1: The beggar is bawling for dinars (but might settle for centimes).
column2: 乞丐扯著嗓子討第納爾(不過給點生丁小錢他大概也認了)。

column1: The bird is kept in a locked cabinet in that room.
column2: 那隻鳥被鎖在那房間的櫃子裡。

column1: The black bird does not open.
column2: 那隻黑鳥打不開。

column1: The black bird is too heavy to Fetch. You wonder what it could be made of that would make it so heavy.
column2: 那隻黑鳥太重,「取物」術搬不動。你納悶它到底是什麼做的,怎會這麼重。

column1: The blazing desert sun beats down fiercely on your head.
column2: 炙熱的沙漠驕陽狠狠灼烤著你的頭頂。

column1: The blazing desert sun makes it impossible to rest here. The withered tree provides no shade at all.
column2: 炙熱的沙漠驕陽讓你無法在此歇息,那棵枯樹遮不了半點蔭。

column1: The book itself is labeled "Warrior's Diary." You decide to keep it and use it for your own notes.
column2: 這本書上頭標著「戰士日記」。你決定留下它,拿來記自己的筆記。

column1: The box is too heavy for your Fetch spell.
column2: 這箱子太重,你的「取物」術搬不動。

column1: The brass jug is too heavy for your Fetch spell.
column2: 這黃銅壺太重,你的「取物」術搬不動。

column1: The bread is first fried in olive oil.
column2: 麵包要先用橄欖油煎過。

column1: The Brigand doesn't respond. That probably means he wants your money AND your life.
column2: 那強盜沒有回應。這多半表示他既要你的錢,也要你的命。

column1: The Brigand is finally down for good. You wonder how a Brigand ended up in the city in the first place.
column2: 那強盜終於徹底倒下了。你納悶,一個強盜當初究竟是怎麼混進城裡的。

column1: The Brigand no longer has anything worth taking on him.
column2: 那強盜身上再也沒什麼值得拿的了。

column1: The city walls are too slick to scale and you'd only get arrested if you tried.
column2: 城牆太滑,爬不上去,真要試也只會被抓。

column1: The coins of Shapeir are stamped with an image of the Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May He Rule Forever!).
column2: 夏皮爾的錢幣上鑄著蘇丹哈倫·拉希德的肖像(願他永世統治!)。

column1: The combat copilot is not available on the highest difficulty setting.
column2: 在最高難度設定下,無法使用戰鬥輔助。

column1: The crates are nailed shut. You have neither the time nor the patience to open them.
column2: 這些木箱都被釘死了。你既沒時間、也沒耐性去撬開它們。

column1: The Creature of Water prevents any from approaching the fountain. I fear we will soon have no water.
column2: 那水之造物不准任何人靠近噴泉。我怕我們很快就要沒水了。

column1: The creature's tough, armor-like exterior is certain to render any bare-hand attacks completely useless.
column2: 這生物堅硬如甲的外殼,肯定讓任何徒手攻擊全然無效。

column1: The desert heat appears to be getting to you, Hero-friend.
column2: 沙漠的酷熱似乎讓你有點吃不消了,英雄朋友。

column1: The desert is a very dangerous place for any man.
column2: 對任何人而言,沙漠都是個極其危險的地方。

column1: The Djinni is not interested in any of your possessions.
column2: 鎮尼對你的任何東西都不感興趣。

column1: The door appears to be locked.
column2: 門似乎鎖著。

column1: The door defies your attempt to open it.
column2: 這扇門任你怎麼弄都打不開。

column1: The door does not seem to be magic, but there is a glow behind it.
column2: 這門看來不帶魔法,門後卻透著一抹微光。

column1: The door has swung out too far. You can't reach it from the pathway.
column2: 門開得太外了,你從通道上構不著它。

column1: The door is already open.
column2: 門已經開著了。

column1: The door is barred from the other side. Your Open spell has no effect.
column2: 門從另一側閂住了。你的「開啟」術毫無作用。

column1: The door is barred. Your Open spell has no effect.
column2: 門閂著。你的「開啟」術毫無作用。

column1: The door is locked tight, and the sound of your knocks echoes hollowly in the alley, without response.
column2: 門鎖得死死的,你的敲門聲在巷子裡空洞地迴響,無人應答。

column1: The druggist seems to keep busy at his trade.
column2: 那藥販似乎一直忙著做他的生意。

column1: The dry heat of the desert seems to have stunted the plant's growth.
column2: 沙漠乾燥的酷熱似乎遏止了這株植物的生長。

column1: The duty of a guard in Shapeir is to make certain all people are safe from violence or ill-doing.
column2: 夏皮爾衛兵的職責,是確保所有人免遭暴力與惡行。

column1: The Earth Elemental is a hulking mass of rock whose very footsteps make the city shake.
column2: 土元素精靈是一團龐然的岩石,光是踏步就能撼動整座城。

column1: The Elemental dodges easily in the open plaza.
column2: 在開闊的廣場上,那元素精靈輕易就閃開了。

column1: The Elemental earth keeps slipping through your hands.
column2: 那元素之土不斷從你指縫間滑落。

column1: The Elemental is not in a good position for that.
column2: 那元素精靈的位置不適合那麼做。

column1: The Elemental's eyes aren't any more watery than usual.
column2: 那元素精靈的眼睛並不比平常更濕潤。

column1: The eunuch guard, Abu, paces back and forth.
column2: 那名宦官衛兵阿布來回踱步。

column1: The eunuch looks like a formidable foe.
column2: 那宦官看起來是個難纏的對手。

column1: The Fire Elemental seems to like that a lot.
column2: 火元素精靈似乎很受用。

column1: The flagstones of the street are in sordid condition, just like the rest of the city.
column2: 街上的石板骯髒不堪,跟這城裡其他地方一個樣。

column1: The flagstones of the street feel smooth.
column2: 街上的石板摸起來光滑。

column1: The flailing, bloodthirsty swordsman looks like he means business.
column2: 那揮刀亂砍、嗜血的劍客,看來是來真的。

column1: The footwear isn't really different from what you currently have.
column2: 這鞋子跟你現在穿的其實沒什麼兩樣。

column1: The Force Bolt is just absorbed. It's obviously not effective against liquid life forms.
column2: 「力箭」被直接吸收了。對液態生命體顯然起不了作用。

column1: The fountain is the heart of the city.
column2: 噴泉是這座城市的心臟。

column1: The "fruit" in the barrel is covered with a thick layer of mildew. Yuck!
column2: 桶裡那些「水果」蒙著厚厚一層黴。噁!

column1: The Ghoul's head is probably the most disgusting thing you've ever had the misfortune of carrying around.
column2: 食屍鬼的頭,大概是你這輩子倒楣帶在身上的東西裡最噁心的一個。

column1: The Ghoul would be delighted to shake hands with you. And suck your strength and stamina while doing so.
column2: 食屍鬼會很樂意跟你握手——順便在握手時吸乾你的力氣與耐力。

column1: The graffiti communicates to you in a different way.
column2: 那塗鴉以另一種方式向你傳達訊息。

column1: The Griffin has many feathers. Perhaps you can grab one of them.
column2: 獅鷲有許多羽毛。或許你能抓走其中一根。

column1: The Griffin's feather is grey-striped with silver at the tip. There is brown fur attached to the quill.
column2: 這根獅鷲羽毛帶著灰色條紋,尖端泛銀,羽根上還黏著棕色的絨毛。

column1: The guard does not respond.
column2: 衛兵沒有回應。

column1: The guard gives you a quick nod and then walks on.
column2: 衛兵朝你飛快地點了個頭,便走開了。

column1: The guard gives you a vicious glare before moving on.
column2: 衛兵惡狠狠地瞪了你一眼,才繼續往前走。

column1: The guard looks even more stone-faced than usual. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 那衛兵的臉比平常更鐵青。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: The guard looks official.
column2: 那衛兵一副公差模樣。

column1: The guards are too busy guarding the palace to listen to you.
column2: 衛兵們忙著守衛王宮,沒空理你。

column1: The guards march ceaselessly before the gates to the Palace.
column2: 衛兵在王宮大門前不停地來回踏步。

column1: The guards of this city look like tough, well-disciplined fighters.
column2: 這城裡的衛兵看起來是一群剽悍、紀律嚴明的戰士。

column1: The guards stand motionless at the gates.
column2: 衛兵一動不動地守在大門口。

column1: The Guild Hall was the first place Shema and I visited upon our arrival in Spielburg.
column2: 公會大廳是雪瑪和我抵達史畢柏格後造訪的第一個地方。

column1: The hot desert air scorches your throat as you breathe.
column2: 你每吸一口氣,熾熱的沙漠空氣便灼著你的喉嚨。

column1: THE ICONS[[INVENTORY: Choose this when you want to view and select from the items you're carrying.
column2: 圖示說明[[物品欄:想查看並從你攜帶的物品中挑選時,選這個。

column1: The incense is finely ground and has a sweet smelling scent.
column2: 這薰香研磨得很細,散發著甜美的香氣。

column1: The invisibility spell on the door has already been removed. Your spell has no further effects.
column2: 門上的隱形咒已經解除了。你的法術不再有任何作用。

column1: The jerky is prepared with only the finest, most succulent, rock-flattened lizards.
column2: 這肉乾只選用最上等、最多汁、用石頭壓扁的蜥蜴製成。

column1: The jewelry I sell will make the woman you adore the most beautiful person in the world.
column2: 我賣的珠寶,能讓你傾慕的女子成為世上最美的人。

column1: The Katta are again afraid to set up their shops. The Creature of Earth grows ever more powerful.
column2: 卡塔族又不敢開張做生意了。那土之造物越來越強大。

column1: The Katta are most thankful that you have come to Shapeir.
column2: 卡塔族對你來到夏皮爾感激不盡。

column1: The Katta gave you this sapphire pin in thanks for your service to their kind.
column2: 卡塔族送你這枚藍寶石別針,感謝你為他們族人效力。

column1: The Katta is thin and it looks like he's recently been beaten.
column2: 這卡塔族人瘦削不堪,看樣子近來挨過打。

column1: The Katta just stares suspiciously at you.
column2: 那卡塔族人只是滿腹狐疑地盯著你。

column1: The Katta merchant has no need of that.
column2: 那卡塔族商人不需要那個。

column1: The Katta merchants could not set up in the plaza outside because of the fire this morning.
column2: 因為今早那場火,卡塔族商人沒法在外頭的廣場擺攤。

column1: Shema just gave you this spare change of clothes for Raseir. You do not want to store them now.
column2: 雪瑪才剛給你這套去拉希爾用的備用衣裳。你現在不想收起來。

column1: The ice is now very relaxed.
column2: 那冰塊現在很放鬆。

column1: The Katta nods to acknowledge your presence.
column2: 那卡塔族人點頭示意,表示注意到你了。

column1: The Katta quickly bows to acknowledge your greeting and then moves on.
column2: 那卡塔族人迅速一鞠躬回應你的問候,便走開了。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn is in the Gate Plaza.
column2: 「卡塔之尾客棧」在城門廣場。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn is in this very plaza.
column2: 「卡塔之尾客棧」就在這座廣場上。

column1: The kitchen is closed already. Your tummy will have to wait until tomorrow morning.
column2: 廚房已經打烊了。你的肚子只能等到明天早上。

column1: The lamp burns brightly, lighting this otherwise darkened dead-end.
column2: 那盞燈燃得正旺,照亮了這條原本漆黑的死巷。

column1: The last thing this window needs is a set of greasy fingerprints smeared across its surface.
column2: 這扇窗最不需要的,就是表面被抹上一排油膩的指紋。

column1: The leather merchant frowns at you.
column2: 那皮革商朝你皺眉。

column1: The leonine creature before you looks ferocious and kindly at the same time.
column2: 你面前這頭獅形生物,看起來既兇猛又和善。

column1: The Liontaur looks calmly back at you.
column2: 那獅人平靜地回望你。

column1: The magic lamp is not useful here.
column2: 魔法神燈在這兒派不上用場。

column1: The Magic Shop owner might help you with such questions.
column2: 魔法店的老闆或許能解答你這類問題。

column1: The man gives you a scowl that could sink a sub.
column2: 那人朝你擺出一張足以擊沉潛艇的臭臉。

column1: The man is built just like an acrobat.
column2: 那人一身體格活像個雜耍藝人。

column1: The man looks to be dazed for some reason.
column2: 那人不知怎地一臉茫然。

column1: The mechanic doesn't look like he's willing to interact with you, much less shake hands.
column2: 那技工看來無意搭理你,更別說握手了。

column1: The memory of a poet is long for those who aid him.
column2: 詩人對助他之人,記性可長著呢。

column1: The merchant looks at you strangely.
column2: 那商人古怪地看著你。

column1: The mere thought of putting your hand in there disgusts you.
column2: 光想到要把手伸進那裡頭,你就一陣噁心。

column1: The message reads:[["Misfortune will arise when the dinar hits the fountain."
column2: 上頭寫著:[[「當第納爾落入噴泉,厄運將至。」

column1: The message reads:[["Rent your own Harem at Lou's."
column2: 上頭寫著:[[「到老盧家,租你專屬的後宮。」

column1: The message reads:[["Those who dare to walk the fiery road will not return unscathed."
column2: 上頭寫著:[[「膽敢踏上烈火之路者,難以全身而退。」

column1: The message reads:[["Those who turn onto the dark, starry path will face great peril."
column2: 上頭寫著:[[「轉入那條漆黑星路者,將面臨莫大凶險。」

column1: The musician has no need of your Spielburgian money.
column2: 那樂師不需要你的史畢柏格錢幣。

column1: The musician nods and smiles while he plays.
column2: 那樂師一邊演奏,一邊點頭微笑。

column1: The musician seems too busy to answer you.
column2: 那樂師似乎忙得無暇答理你。

column1: The musician would not be interested in that.
column2: 那樂師對那個不會感興趣。

column1: Then honey is poured upon it.
column2: 接著淋上蜂蜜。

column1: Then it is quickly cooked over the brazier there.
column2: 然後在那邊的炭爐上迅速烤熟。

column1: Then you may leave, mere user of magic.
column2: 那你可以走了,區區一個耍魔法的。

column1: Then you must choose another sponsor.
column2: 那你得另選一位引薦人。

column1: The odd character at this stand seems vaguely familiar.
column2: 這攤子上那個古怪傢伙,看著有點面熟。

column1: The once beautiful pot now lies in ruins.
column2: 那只曾經精美的陶罐,如今已成一堆碎片。

column1: The only response from the Air Elemental is a low, ominous hum.
column2: 風元素精靈唯一的回應,是一陣低沉而不祥的嗡鳴。

column1: The only thing it can tell you is that it is in a state beyond repair.
column2: 它唯一能告訴你的,就是它已經壞到沒法修了。

column1: The only thing they seem interested in is eating straw.
column2: 牠們似乎唯一感興趣的,就是吃草料。

column1: The palace doors are barred securely from inside.
column2: 王宮的大門從內側牢牢閂住了。

column1: The Palace is a marvelous place to work.
column2: 王宮是個極好的做事之處。

column1: The Palace of the Emir looms ominously over the city.
column2: 埃米爾的宮殿不祥地高踞於城市之上。

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is a truly magnificent sight.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿著實是一派恢宏景象。

column1: The pin is a token of the Katta's trust in you, O Hero.
column2: 英雄啊,這枚別針是卡塔族對你信任的信物。

column1: The place is the window on your left as you first enter the Fountain Plaza from here.
column2: 那地方,是你從這兒初進噴泉廣場時左手邊的那扇窗。

column1: The plant seems to have adapted to the desert heat.
column2: 這株植物似乎已經適應了沙漠的酷熱。

column1: The plaza is too open an area to capture and contain a Fire Elemental.
column2: 廣場太空曠了,沒法捕捉並困住火元素精靈。

column1: The pleasure is all mine, Hero.
column2: 這是我的榮幸,英雄。

column1: The Poet is bothered more by the thief than by the loss of money.
column2: 比起損失的錢,詩人更在意那個小偷。

column1: The Poet Omar is honored by your greetings and wishes you the same in return.
column2: 詩人奧瑪爾很榮幸收到你的問候,也以同樣的祝福回敬你。

column1: The pot you obtained from Toshur is carefully crafted and very smooth.
column2: 你從托舒爾那兒得來的陶罐做工精細、十分光滑。

column1: The pot you obtained from Toshur is colorful and very smooth.
column2: 你從托舒爾那兒得來的陶罐色彩繽紛、十分光滑。

column1: The presence of fetid trash piles makes this a less than pleasant area to navigate through.
column2: 一堆堆惡臭的垃圾,讓這地方走起來實在不怎麼舒坦。

column1: The quantity I have given you should suffice.
column2: 我給你的份量應該夠了。

column1: The rations are tasteless but filling.
column2: 這乾糧雖然無味,倒挺管飽。

column1: There are merchants for almost anything in this town.
column2: 這鎮上幾乎什麼東西都有商人在賣。

column1: There are no clothes hanging around here. Besides, you still have your own.
column2: 這附近沒晾著衣服。再說,你自己的還在呢。

column1: There are no games to restore.
column2: 沒有可讀取的存檔。

column1: There are no sounds coming from beyond this door.
column2: 這門後沒有半點聲響。

column1: There are too many guards to fight.
column2: 衛兵太多了,打不過。

column1: There doesn't seem to be anyone on the other side.
column2: 另一側似乎沒有人。

column1: The presence of fetid trash piles make this a less than pleasant area to navigate through.
column2: 一堆堆惡臭的垃圾,讓這地方走起來實在不怎麼舒坦。

column1: There. I do for you what I would not for another. This is a gift for all you have done for the city.
column2: 好了。我為你做的,是別人求都求不來的。這是給你的禮物,謝你為這座城所做的一切。

column1: There is a certain irony in the fact that you partially returned the favor.
column2: 你算是部分回報了這份人情,這事透著幾分諷刺。

column1: There is a magical aura around the door.
column2: 門的四周縈繞著一股魔法氣息。

column1: There is a strong enchantment on the beast and upon the cage.
column2: 那野獸與牢籠上都施著強大的魔咒。

column1: There is incredibly powerful magic going on in the room beyond the door at the top of the stairs.
column2: 樓梯頂端那扇門後的房裡,正進行著無比強大的魔法。

column1: There is no answer to your knocking.
column2: 你敲門,無人應答。

column1: There is no magical trap or lock on the cage door. Your Trigger spell has no effect.
column2: 籠門上沒有魔法陷阱或鎖。你的「觸發」術毫無作用。

column1: There is no need for that. I am a poet rich in spirit already.
column2: 不必了。我是個精神上已然富足的詩人。

column1: There is no need to return it yet, Hero-friend. Please keep it until you decide to end your stay here.
column2: 還不必歸還,英雄朋友。請留著它,直到你決定結束在此的停留。

column1: There is no noise coming from beyond the door. It doesn't sound like the residents are home.
column2: 門後沒有半點聲響。聽起來屋裡的人不在家。

column1: There is no response from the inside.
column2: 裡頭沒有回應。

column1: There is nothing in your storage chest to retrieve.
column2: 你的儲物箱裡沒有可取出的東西。

column1: There is no time to rest. You have a city to save!
column2: 沒空休息。你還有一座城要救!

column1: There may be something in this city worth uprooting, but it isn't the local vines.
column2: 這城裡或許有什麼值得連根拔除,但絕不是這些當地的藤蔓。

column1: There's a thick layer of dust on the sconce.
column2: 那壁燈座上積了厚厚一層灰。

column1: There's a variety of boots for sale at this stand.
column2: 這攤子上賣著各式各樣的靴子。

column1: The residence has been abandoned. Nobody will answer your calls.
column2: 這宅子已經荒廢了,沒人會應你的呼喚。

column1: There's no keyhole on the outside of this door to pick.
column2: 這門外側沒有可以撬的鑰匙孔。

column1: There's no need. He's already being paid for his services here by Shameen.
column2: 不必了。他在這兒的服務,沙明已經付過錢了。

column1: There's no need to drop more incense -- the Fire Elemental is well inside the alley now.
column2: 不必再丟薰香了——火元素精靈現在已經好好待在巷子裡了。

column1: There's no need to go around buying such trinkets unless you're planning on wooing someone.
column2: 除非你打算追求誰,否則沒必要到處買這種小玩意兒。

column1: There's no need to go licking someone else's boots.
column2: 沒必要去舔別人的靴子。

column1: There's no need to purchase any more jewelry from the merchant.
column2: 沒必要再跟那商人買更多珠寶了。

column1: There's no need to throw things around an empty shop.
column2: 沒必要在一家空店裡亂扔東西。

column1: There's no point in resting here when your own room is a few steps away.
column2: 你自己的房間就在幾步之遙,何必在這兒歇。

column1: There's no point in trying that here. The door is obviously magical and has no lock to pick.
column2: 在這兒試那個沒用。這門明顯帶魔法,根本沒鎖可撬。

column1: There's no reason to return the bellows to Issur. Besides, you may still need them.
column2: 沒理由把風箱還給伊蘇。再說,你說不定還用得上。

column1: There's nothing more inside.
column2: 裡頭沒別的了。

column1: There's nothing to do there.
column2: 那兒沒什麼好做的。

column1: There's nothing to Fetch there.
column2: 那兒沒什麼好「取」的。

column1: There's nothing to open there.
column2: 那兒沒什麼好開的。

column1: There's nothing worth fetching.
column2: 沒什麼值得取的。

column1: There's no time for questions. You've got to do something.
column2: 沒空問東問西。你得做點什麼。

column1: There's no time for that now, Hero!
column2: 現在沒空管那個,英雄!

column1: There's no time to rest now.
column2: 現在沒空休息。

column1: There's not much more in stock that's of any use to you.
column2: 庫存裡再沒什麼對你有用的了。

column1: There's no water left in your waterskin.
column2: 你的水袋裡一滴水也不剩了。

column1: There's no way you can reach Ad Avis and there is a magical barrier surrounding the room.
column2: 你根本碰不到阿德·阿維斯,何況這房間四周還圍著一道魔法屏障。

column1: There's only silence coming from the other side.
column2: 另一側只傳來一片寂靜。

column1: There's still some earth left in this worn case, but there's no sign of any plant life.
column2: 這只破舊的盆裡還剩些泥土,卻不見半點植物的跡象。

column1: There was talk that the storm was magical in nature and was conjured up.
column2: 有人說那場風暴本質上是魔法,是被召喚出來的。

column1: There will be a caravan to Raseir in 1 day.
column2: 一天後將有一支前往拉希爾的商隊。

column1: The rock is made of red sandstone.
column2: 這塊石頭是紅砂岩做的。

column1: The rock is shaped like the head of a Griffin.
column2: 這塊石頭形狀像獅鷲的頭。

column1: The rock looks, predictably, much like any other desert rock.
column2: 不出所料,這塊石頭看起來跟別的沙漠石頭沒兩樣。

column1: The rock reflects the desert heat.
column2: 這塊石頭反射著沙漠的熱氣。

column1: The rocks shake, but they are still well-supported at the base.
column2: 那些岩石搖晃著,基座卻依然穩固。

column1: The sapphire pin has a value of 500 dinars. Would you care to bargain?
column2: 這枚藍寶石別針價值五百第納爾。你想討價還價嗎?

column1: The Saurus can't hear you from here.
column2: 從這兒索魯斯聽不見你。

column1: The Saurus meat is first dipped in spicy paprika.
column2: 索魯斯肉要先裹上一層辣紅椒粉。

column1: The save directory is full. You must replace an existing game:
column2: 存檔目錄已滿,你必須覆蓋一個現有的存檔:

column1: The searing desert heat seems to draw the water right out of you.
column2: 灼人的沙漠酷熱,彷彿要把你體內的水分全榨乾。

column1: These impressive hedges have been cleverly manicured to conform with the upward direction of the walls.
column2: 這些氣派的樹籬經過巧手修剪,順著牆向上的走勢成形。

column1: The Seller of Sauruses will be here tomorrow. Perhaps you can purchase one then.
column2: 賣索魯斯的明天會來。或許到時你能買一頭。

column1: These look like beautiful but toxic oleander flowers.
column2: 這些看起來是美麗卻有毒的夾竹桃花。

column1: These make good salad for dinner. That be very kind of you.
column2: 這些拿來當晚餐的沙拉正好。你真是太好心了。

column1: These ornate scimitars make poor weapons, but a valuable decoration.
column2: 這些華麗的彎刀當兵器不行,當擺飾倒挺值錢。

column1: These traditional windows are arch-shaped and have been built into the wall.
column2: 這些傳統樣式的窗子呈拱形,嵌在牆裡。

column1: These traditional wooden windows are square-shaped and have been built into the wall.
column2: 這些傳統的木窗呈方形,嵌在牆裡。

column1: These vines seem to have been cut off near the root and are now withering away in the harsh sun.
column2: 這些藤蔓似乎被攔腰齊根斬斷,如今正在烈日下枯萎。

column1: The shiny brass lamp is engraved on the bottom: 'Aladdin Lamp Co.'
column2: 這盞亮晶晶的銅燈底部刻著:「阿拉丁神燈公司」。

column1: The shop owner glares back at you. He looks strong and decidedly unfriendly.
column2: 店主回瞪你一眼。他看起來壯實,而且擺明了不友善。

column1: The shutters are barely managing to remain in place. Don't make matters worse.
column2: 那百葉窗勉強還掛在原處。別把事情弄得更糟。

column1: The shutters are just fine the way they are.
column2: 那百葉窗就這樣挺好的。

column1: The shutters on this window look ready to fall out of their hinges at any moment.
column2: 這扇窗的百葉,看起來隨時會從鉸鏈上掉下來。

column1: The sign looks tattered and worse for wear.
column2: 那招牌看起來破爛不堪、飽經風霜。

column1: The sign of the Scorpion.
column2: 天蠍之象。

column1: The soles of your boots seem unable to withstand any more damage.
column2: 你靴底似乎再也經不起任何磨損了。

column1: The sound of your footsteps seem to echo "water" in your ears.
column2: 你的腳步聲在耳中迴響,彷彿在喊著「水」。

column1: The spell does not seem to affect the wind.
column2: 這法術似乎對風起不了作用。

column1: The spell on the room prevents the powder from igniting.
column2: 這房間的咒法使得粉末無法點燃。

column1: The star pattern known as the Dragon.
column2: 被稱作「巨龍」的星象。

column1: The stars here appear to form a Dark Hand.
column2: 這裡的星辰似乎排成了一隻「黑手」。

column1: The strong bars prevent any would-be intruders from entering.
column2: 那堅固的柵欄擋住了任何意圖闖入者。

column1: The stuff inside the barrel would have no trouble crawling out on its own if it was so inclined.
column2: 桶裡那玩意兒只要願意,自己爬出來毫不費勁。

column1: The Sultan (May He Reign Forever!) is truly wise and powerful.
column2: 蘇丹(願他永世統治!)著實英明而強大。

column1: The sum doesn't balance. COD or DOA. Pay or go away.
column2: 帳對不上。貨到付款,否則一拍兩散。給錢,不然走人。

column1: The summer heat is dangerous for those who are not used to it.
column2: 對不習慣的人來說,這夏日酷熱十分危險。

column1: The sun is bright and the day is hot already this morning.
column2: 今早陽光刺眼,天氣已經熱了起來。

column1: The supernatural being looks like a skeleton with pieces of rotting flesh still attached to its bones.
column2: 那超自然之物像具骷髏,骨頭上還黏著一塊塊腐肉。

column1: The surgeon general warns...
column2: 衛生署長警告……

column1: The swords are too firmly attached to the wall to Fetch.
column2: 那些劍牢牢釘在牆上,「取物」術取不下來。

column1: The taste will sell itself.
column2: 這味道自會替自己招攬生意。

column1: The tenants are either not at home or unwilling to let you in.
column2: 屋裡的人若不是不在家,就是不願讓你進門。

column1: The Terrorsaurus is too stupid to understand you.
column2: 那頭恐魯斯蠢得聽不懂你的話。

column1: The Terrorsaurus looks vicious, mean, and quite hungry.
column2: 那頭恐魯斯看起來兇狠、惡毒,而且相當飢餓。

column1: The tingling sensation is gone. It seems the unguent wore off.
column2: 那刺麻感消失了。看來藥膏的效力過了。

column1: The townsperson doesn't seem to understand you.
column2: 那市民似乎聽不懂你的話。

column1: The townsperson looks nondescript.
column2: 那市民其貌不揚,毫無特徵。

column1: The trash can's aroma suits its appearance very well.
column2: 那垃圾桶的氣味,跟它的賣相還真相稱。

column1: The tree does not respond.
column2: 那棵樹沒有反應。

column1: The tree rustles its leaves.
column2: 那棵樹沙沙地擺動著葉子。

column1: The trip from Shapeir to Sangerhafen had already taken several months and winter was approaching rapidly.
column2: 從夏皮爾到桑格哈芬這趟路已走了好幾個月,寒冬又迅速逼近。

column1: The wall appears to have a spell placed upon it.
column2: 那面牆似乎被施了咒。

column1: The wall at the end of the alley appears to have been damaged.
column2: 巷子盡頭那面牆似乎受了損。

column1: The wall is carrying a torch for you.
column2: 那面牆對你「情有獨鍾」(還幫你舉著火把呢)。

column1: The Warrior's Diary contains your recorded experiences during battle.
column2: 「戰士日記」記錄著你在戰鬥中的種種經歷。

column1: The warrior woman cradles baby Simba in her arms.
column2: 那女戰士懷裡搖著小辛巴。

column1: The warrior woman stands tall and proud.
column2: 那女戰士站得挺拔而自豪。

column1: The Water Elemental destroys all who go near her.
column2: 水元素精靈會毀滅一切靠近她的人。

column1: The Water Elemental doesn't seem to be affected by your spell.
column2: 水元素精靈似乎不受你的法術影響。

column1: The Water Elemental in the fountain of town is the greatest of misfortunes.
column2: 水元素精靈進了城中的噴泉,正是天大的災禍。

column1: The weapons at this stand resemble the ones the desert brigands use.
column2: 這攤子上的兵器,跟沙漠強盜用的那些很像。

column1: The wind grows stronger, and the merchants had great difficulty setting up their stands today.
column2: 風越颳越大,今天商人們擺攤都吃足了苦頭。

column1: The wind no longer blows as fiercely as before.
column2: 風不再像先前颳得那麼猛了。

column1: The window doesn't open up to your words.
column2: 那扇窗不為你的話語而開。

column1: The window is always open. The climate here is always hot.
column2: 這扇窗總是開著,這兒的氣候向來炎熱。

column1: The window is barred from the inside.
column2: 那扇窗從內側閂住了。

column1: The window lacks curtains or shutters you can fiddle with.
column2: 這扇窗沒有可供你擺弄的窗簾或百葉。

column1: The wise old Dervish gives you a funny look, but says nothing. Now you just feel silly!
column2: 那睿智的老苦行僧古怪地瞅了你一眼,卻什麼也沒說。這下你只覺得自己很蠢!

column1: The Wizard's Institute of Technocery is for real Wizards rather than the real world!
column2: 巫師技術學院是給真正的巫師待的,可不是給「真實世界」待的!

column1: The woman is very attractive with her dark, curly hair and lovely figure. She smiles invitingly at you.
column2: 那女子一頭深色鬈髮、身段曼妙,十分迷人,正朝你嫣然一笑、頗為撩人。

column1: The wooden bars prevent the door from being opened, even if there was someone on the other side.
column2: 那木栅擋住了門,就算另一側有人也開不了。

column1: The words of departure are what I do hear,[  Farewell 'til the next time you lend me your ear.
column2: 我聽見的,是道別之語,[  就此別過,待你下回再側耳傾聽。

column1: They do not respond.
column2: 它們沒有反應。

column1: They obviously improve your looks. Zayishah just giggles at you.
column2: 它們顯然讓你好看了些。札伊莎只是對你咯咯直笑。

column1: They probably don't want to be petted.
column2: 牠們大概不想被摸。

column1: They travel very well.
column2: 牠們很耐遠行。

column1: This beautiful emerald-studded silver bowl was once the property of the silversmith Abu bin Ma'amar.
column2: 這只鑲著翡翠的精美銀碗,曾是銀匠阿布·賓·馬阿瑪爾的財產。

column1: This business has no major hang-ups... or does it?
column2: 這買賣沒什麼大「掛」礙……還是有?

column1: This does seem like a good place. You curl up near the pool and soon fall asleep.
column2: 這地方看來不錯。你在水池邊蜷起身子,很快便睡著了。

column1: This drink sizzles in the glass.
column2: 這飲料在杯子裡嘶嘶作響。

column1: This guy is the spitting-image of Prince Ali-Ababwa.
column2: 這傢伙活脫脫就是阿里·阿巴布瓦王子的翻版。

column1: This guy looks like he could play the piano, but strangely.
column2: 這傢伙看起來會彈鋼琴,只是彈得有點怪。

column1: This guy looks like he's been in the desert too long.
column2: 這傢伙看起來在沙漠裡待得太久了。

column1: This is a fine sword from the Weapon Shop of Issur.
column2: 這是一把出自伊蘇兵器店的好劍。

column1: This is a leather waterskin containing the essence of the Water Elemental.
column2: 這是一個皮水袋,裡頭封著水元素精靈的精華。

column1: This is not a good opportunity to use your rope.
column2: 現在不是用繩子的好時機。

column1: This is not a good time for that.
column2: 現在做那個不是時候。

column1: This is not a good time to practice your throwing.
column2: 現在不是練投擲的好時機。

column1: This is not a safe place to rest.
column2: 這裡不是個安全的歇腳處。

column1: This is not a safe place to sleep.
column2: 這裡不是個安全的睡覺地方。

column1: This isn't a good moment to socialize.
column2: 現在不是交際攀談的好時機。

column1: This isn't the best place to practice your Levitate spell.
column2: 這裡不是練「飄浮」術的好地方。

column1: This is the harem girl's hand mirror.
column2: 這是那後宮女子的手鏡。

column1: This is the last page.
column2: 這是最後一頁。

column1: This is your title and registration for your trusty riding Saurus.
column2: 這是你那頭可靠坐騎索魯斯的所有權證與登記文件。

column1: This key can't be assigned to a combat action.
column2: 這個按鍵不能指派給戰鬥動作。

column1: This key doesn't fit that lock.
column2: 這把鑰匙跟那個鎖不合。

column1: This lamp of brass should serve you against the thing of fire. It is now your own.
column2: 這盞銅燈該能助你對付那團火之物。如今它歸你了。

column1: This leather purse uses a drawstring for closure.
column2: 這個皮錢袋用束口繩封口。

column1: This make very fine wall trophy. Asante, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 這拿來當牆上的戰利品很不錯。Asante(謝謝),史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: This merchant has various vests for sale.
column2: 這商人賣著各式背心。

column1: This merchant offers purses for sale.
column2: 這商人賣著錢袋。

column1: This merchant sells clay pots.
column2: 這商人賣陶罐。

column1: This ornament is the black figure of a bird, a falcon.
column2: 這件飾品是一隻黑色鳥形雕像,一隻獵鷹。

column1: This ornate window has lost much of its original beauty.
column2: 這扇華麗的窗,已失去大半原有的美。

column1: This part of the wall seems to be in particularly bad shape.
column2: 這一段牆似乎特別殘破。

column1: This person isn't interested in the object you offer.
column2: 這人對你拿出的東西不感興趣。

column1: This residence appears to have been abandoned, as its door has been barred with several wooden planks.
column2: 這宅子似乎已遭遺棄,門被好幾塊木板封死了。

column1: This sign symbolizes the business owner's trade.
column2: 這招牌象徵著店主所做的行當。

column1: This square, flat box is made from material that somewhat resembles paper, except it is harder.
column2: 這只方扁的盒子,材質有點像紙,只是更硬些。

column1: This stand sells golden bracelets, rings, and necklaces.
column2: 這攤子賣金手鐲、戒指和項鍊。

column1: This street has been barricaded off. The sign reads: "Road closed by order of Khaveen."
column2: 這條街被封了起來。告示寫著:「奉哈維因之命,此路封閉。」

column1: This torch is not the light of your life.
column2: 這支火把可不是你的「此生摯愛」。

column1: This vase houses a lovely plant.
column2: 這只花瓶裡養著一株可愛的植物。

column1: This wealthy-looking Katta is Tiram, the carpet merchant.
column2: 這個看來富裕的卡塔族人,是賣地毯的商人提拉姆。

column1: This will only take me one second.
column2: 我一秒鐘就好。

column1: This will take many hours, so you will need to come back some other day.
column2: 這得花上好幾個鐘頭,你得改天再來。

column1: This window frame has seen better days.
column2: 這扇窗框早已風光不再。

column1: Those that can't, tell others what to do?
column2: 辦不到的人,就只會指使別人怎麼做?

column1: Through this door lies the other side.
column2: 這門之後,便是另一側。

column1: Through this door's keyhole, a constant mournful wail fills the room.
column2: 透過這門的鑰匙孔,一陣不止歇的哀號充塞著整個房間。

column1: Throwing knives when guards are around is not the wisest course of action.
column2: 衛兵在旁時擲刀,可不是什麼明智之舉。

column1: Throwing rocks when guards are around is not the wisest course of action.
column2: 衛兵在旁時擲石,可不是什麼明智之舉。

column1: Throw it quickly at your target. Try not to miss. It will do a lot of damage when it strikes and ignites.
column2: 快把它擲向目標,別失手。它擊中並炸開時威力可不小。

column1: Today is good day for fighting practice. Every day is good day for practice.
column2: 今天是練武的好日子。其實天天都是練武的好日子。

column1: To get past the guards outside, you have only to make the 'sign.'
column2: 想通過外頭的衛兵,你只需比出那個「暗號」。

column1: Tomorrow night is your last night. You'll get your final orders then. Be seeing you.
column2: 「明晚是你的最後一晚。到時你會收到最終指令。回頭見。」

column1: Tonight's meal is honeyed lamb with almonds and curried chicken. It is hoped you will enjoy yourself.
column2: 今晚的菜色是蜜汁杏仁羊肉與咖哩雞。願您吃得盡興。

column1: Top o' the morning to ya! Mar haba! What's new, Pussycat?
column2: 早安啊您吶!Mar haba(你好)!最近如何呀,小貓咪?

column1: To serve you is an honor.
column2: 能為您效勞是一種榮幸。

column1: Toshur is the local pottery merchant.
column2: 托舒爾是本地的陶器商。

column1: Type a name to save as:
column2: 輸入要存檔的名稱:

column1: Uh oh. The guards have found you prowling outside at night. You're immediately taken into custody.
column2: 糟了。衛兵發現你夜裡在外頭遊蕩,你當場就被收押了。

column1: Uh oh. The guards have gotten tired of you hanging around here. You're taken into custody.
column2: 糟了。衛兵受夠了你在這兒晃來晃去,把你收押了。

column1: Uhura greets you with an ominous glare.
column2: 烏胡拉以一記不祥的瞪視迎接你。

column1: Uhura isn't interested in that item.
column2: 烏胡拉對那件東西不感興趣。

column1: Unfortunately, the guard is upon you before your spell takes effect.
column2: 可惜,你的法術還沒生效,衛兵就撲到你面前了。

column1: Unfortunately, your foe is upon you before your spell takes effect.
column2: 可惜,你的法術還沒生效,敵人就撲到你面前了。

column1: Unlike a certain King Edward of Daventry, you don't plan on losing this mirror.
column2: 你可不打算像達文垂的某位愛德華王那樣,把這面鏡子弄丟。

column1: Unlike heads, two layers of unguent are no better than one. But it didn't harm you either.
column2: 跟頭不一樣,藥膏抹兩層並不比一層好,不過抹了也無妨。

column1: Unlike heads, two poison cure pills are no better than one. But the pill does no harm either.
column2: 跟頭不一樣,解毒藥丸吃兩顆並不比一顆好,不過吃了也無妨。

column1: Use the counterslash to retaliate against blockable attacks by your adversary.
column2: 用「反斬」來回擊對手那些可格擋的攻擊。

column1: Use the rising slash to turn the tables on an opponent who relies on high attacks.
column2: 用「上挑斬」來反制慣使上段攻擊的對手。

column1: Very well, if such is your choice. What is it that you wish to know?
column2: 好吧,既然你這麼選。你想知道什麼?

column1: Vines cling to the walls, twisting their way skyward.
column2: 藤蔓攀附在牆上,扭著身子向天空伸展。

column1: Wall: stops two attacks
column2: 壁壘:擋下兩次攻擊

column1: Warning! Don't speak to the awning.
column2: 警告!別跟那遮篷說話。

column1: Warning: items you drop will be lost permanently.
column2: 警告:你丟下的物品將永久消失。

column1: We are happy you have been our guest and even happier you have been our friend.
column2: 你做我們的客人,我們很高興;你做我們的朋友,我們更是欣喜。

column1: We are most honored by your presence at our inn, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 史畢柏格的英雄,您光臨我們的客棧,我們深感榮幸。

column1: We are very fastidious when working, and you will find no Katta hairs in the dates.
column2: 我們做事一絲不苟,你在椰棗裡絕找不到半根卡塔族的毛。

column1: We do not accept intruders in our tower.
column2: 我們的塔不容入侵者。

column1: We do not accept liars in our tower.
column2: 我們的塔不容說謊者。

column1: Welcome back. If you see something that interests you, let me know.
column2: 歡迎回來。你要是看上了什麼,就告訴我。

column1: It would be best to wait until you're back in the main Guild Hall before telling about your stories, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你那些故事,最好等回到公會大廳再說。

column1: I will look into what signs have guided your life thus far, and see how they will affect you in the future.
column2: 我會查查迄今引領你人生的是哪些星象,再看它們將如何影響你的未來。

column1: Most of Spielburg's problems were a great many years old, yet you managed to solve them in such little time.
column2: 史畢柏格的麻煩多半積了許多年,你卻在這麼短的時間內一一化解。

column1: Oh, Effendi. This is by far the best food this lowly beggar has ever tasted. I am in awe of your generosity.
column2: 啊,閣下。這是我這卑微乞丐吃過最好的食物了。您的慷慨令我敬佩不已。

column1: Old pieces of paper have seemingly been carried here by the wind and walked into the pavement by passersby.
column2: 幾張舊紙片像是被風颳到這兒,又被過路人踩進了石板地裡。

column1: Open it and it may fall. Close it and it may fall. Leave it alone and it'll be fine. You decide on option 3.
column2: 開它它可能掉,關它它可能掉,別碰它倒沒事。你選了第三個。

column1: Seeing that you two kids are hitting it off from the start, it sounds like a good time to start heading off.
column2: 瞧你倆小傢伙一見如故,看來我也該識相地走人了。

column1: The awning must have been bright and colorful in the past. Now, it's as dreary as the wall it's attached to.
column2: 這遮篷想必曾經鮮豔奪目。如今,卻跟它依附的那面牆一樣黯淡。

column1: The Brigand doesn't seem to have any cash on him. You do, however, find a small book in one of his pockets.
column2: 那強盜身上似乎沒帶錢。不過,你在他口袋裡找到一本小冊子。

column1: The closet doors are latched closed. Besides, there is nothing inside the closet. You don't need to open it.
column2: 櫥門扣著。再說,櫥裡什麼也沒有,你不必開它。

column1: The snake took a wrong turn one day and wound up listening to a Katta who likes to think he's Benny Goodman.
column2: 那條蛇某天拐錯了彎,結果聽起一個自以為是班尼·古德曼的卡塔族人吹奏。

column1: This is finest souvenir stand in town. I personally guarantee it. This is also only souvenir stand in town.
column2: 這是城裡最棒的紀念品攤,我親自掛保證。這也是城裡唯一的紀念品攤。

column1: Welcome. How may I be of service?
column2: 歡迎。有什麼能為您效勞的?

column1: Welcome. It is good to have a visitor who understands the proper way to enter conversation.
column2: 歡迎。能有一位懂得如何得體開口的訪客,真好。

column1: Welcome. May the stars smile upon you.
column2: 歡迎。願群星向你微笑。

column1: Well, I hope you figure out how to deal with it. This city will soon run out of water.
column2: 好吧,但願你想得出對付它的法子。這座城快沒水了。

column1: Well, that was going nowhere the long way.
column2: 唉,這一通繞下來,還是白忙一場。

column1: Well, you go north until you get to the fountain and you take-a the road west from there.
column2: 這樣啊,你往北走到噴泉,再從那兒走西邊的路。

column1: We shall all die of thirst!
column2: 我們都會渴死的!

column1: We sold the inn we were running at that time so we would have funds to obtain new supplies during the trip.
column2: 我們把當時經營的客棧賣了,好籌些錢在途中採購新的補給。

column1: We were most saddened to hear that the valley was still beset with problems.
column2: 聽聞那山谷仍是麻煩纏身,我們深感哀傷。

column1: We were much relieved when Spielburg valley finally came within sight.
column2: 當史畢柏格谷地終於映入眼簾,我們大大鬆了口氣。

column1: We will keep you in our thoughts.
column2: 我們會把你放在心上。

column1: What are you looking for?
column2: 你在找什麼?

column1: What a small world!
column2: 這世界真小!

column1: What a wonder! Is it a dream? Oh, Effendi, there is no end to your goodness.
column2: 真是奇蹟!莫非是夢?啊,閣下,您的善心沒有止境。

column1: What do you take me for, a common criminal? There is nothing common about me.
column2: 你把我當什麼了,一個尋常罪犯?我這人可一點都不尋常。

column1: What element does the Apothecary specialize in?
column2: 藥師專精於哪一種元素?

column1: What is Ja'Afar's most crucial role for Omar?
column2: 賈法爾對奧瑪爾而言最關鍵的角色是什麼?

column1: What is the element most appropriate to the owner of the Magic Shop?
column2: 哪一種元素最適合魔法店的老闆?

column1: What is your wish, Master?
column2: 你的願望是什麼,主人?

column1: Whatsa matta you? You no like a big deal when you see it?
column2: 你是咋了?好東西擺眼前你還不識貨?

column1: What's a wimp like you asking about EOF for? Only real men join EOF.
column2: 你這種膽小鬼問永恆戰士團做什麼?只有真男人才進得了永恆戰士團。

column1: What you need is a loan. That's where I'll be leaving you until you come up with the cash.
column2: 你需要的是一筆貸款。在你湊出錢來之前,我就跟你聊到這兒。

column1: When it turns into its rat hole?
column2: 等牠鑽回鼠洞的時候?

column1: When you are forced to jump back to avoid damage, your instincts may trigger the leaping lunge.
column2: 當你被迫向後跳開以閃避傷害時,本能或許會觸發一記「躍進突刺」。

column1: When you are in battle against the Elemental, the flame of the sword will aid you.
column2: 當你與元素精靈交戰時,劍上的火焰會助你一臂之力。

column1: When you listen at the door, you hear scratchy whispers.
column2: 你貼著門聽,聽見一陣沙啞的低語。

column1: Which merchant are you trying to talk to?
column2: 你想跟哪個商人說話?

column1: Which of the elements do the wizards of WIT command?
column2: 巫師技術學院的巫師駕馭哪一種元素?

column1: While a barrel ride sounds exciting, there's no waterfall nearby.
column2: 坐桶順流而下聽著刺激,可這附近沒有瀑布。

column1: While the jewelry is lovely, it is also quite useless to you.
column2: 這珠寶雖美,對你卻也相當無用。

column1: While there is a powerful enchantment at work here, your Trigger spell does nothing to help remove it.
column2: 此地雖有強大的魔咒運作,你的「觸發」術卻絲毫無助於解除它。

column2: 這招牌雖有點礙眼,你還是決定讓它留在原處。

column1: Who injured the leg of Rakeesh?
column2: 是誰傷了拉基什的腿?

column1: Who is it that sent you here?
column2: 是誰派你來這兒的?

column1: Who is the companion of Erasmus?
column2: 誰是伊拉斯謨的同伴?

column1: Who needs the Holy Grail when you've got access to this cheat! (Don't tell Arthur).
column2: 有了這作弊碼,誰還稀罕聖杯!(別跟亞瑟王說。)

column1: Why have you sought to enter into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 你為何尋求進入巫師技術學院?

column1: Why have you sought to enter WIT?
column2: 你為何尋求進入巫師技術學院?

column1: Why, I never noticed just how incredibly handsome you are until about a second ago.
column2: 哎呀,直到一秒鐘前,我才注意到你竟是這般俊俏。

column1: Wilmer, a drink for our young friend.
column2: 威爾默,給我們這位年輕朋友來杯喝的。

column1: Wilmer there just does not have quite what it takes to give me what I want.
column2: 那邊那個威爾默,就是沒那本事給我想要的東西。

column1: Without food, you are unable to go on. You collapse and die of malnutrition.
column2: 沒有食物,你撐不下去了。你倒地不起,死於營養不良。

column1: With whom are you trying to speak, Hero?
column2: 你想跟誰說話,英雄?

column1: Yawn. You wonder how the guard can take this kind of activity day in, day out.
column2: 呵欠。你納悶這衛兵怎麼能日復一日忍受這種差事。

column1: Yeah, yeah. What do you want?
column2: 好啦好啦。你想怎樣?

column1: Yeh! Glasses! Maybe that's what you need to stop those migraines. Too bad you don't have any.
column2: 對啦!眼鏡!說不定那正是治你偏頭痛的東西。可惜你一副也沒有。

column1: Yes, even Elsa would not dare venture there alone without her father's guards to fight at her side.
column2: 是啊,連艾莎也不敢在沒有她父親衛兵並肩作戰時,獨自闖那地方。

column1: You already appear to be on his bad side and you'd prefer not to make the situation any worse.
column2: 你似乎已經惹他不快了,還是別把情況弄得更糟為妙。

column1: You already be plenty tired. You recover before we fight more.
column2: 你已經累得不輕了。先恢復恢復,咱們再接著打。

column1: You also relate the tale to your friends about how you made it through the Brigands' lair.
column2: 你也向朋友們講起,你是如何闖過強盜巢穴的。

column1: You ardently describe the Fire Elemental to Harik.
column2: 你熱切地向哈里克描述火元素精靈。

column1: You are a most brave and skilled hero to have made your way through the fortress without being killed.
column2: 你能闖過那座堡壘而不喪命,真是個既勇敢又身手不凡的英雄。

column1: You are carrying 1 basket filled with sand from the desert.
column2: 你帶著 1 個裝滿沙漠沙子的籃子。

column2: 你帶著 1 個大布袋。

column1: You are carrying 1 small clay pot filled with dirt.
column2: 你帶著 1 個裝滿泥土的小陶罐。

column1: You are carrying a flask containing an oily substance to ward off the undead.
column2: 你帶著一只小瓶,裡頭裝著用來驅退亡靈的油狀物。

column1: You are carrying a flask of unguent to ward off the undead.
column2: 你帶著一瓶用來驅退亡靈的藥膏。

column2: 你帶著一個皮水袋,裡頭封著水元素精靈的精華。

column2: 你帶的東西超出了身體能負荷的程度。

column1: You are carrying nothing.
column2: 你什麼也沒帶。

column1: You are carrying Rakeesh's flaming sword 'Soulforge.'
column2: 你帶著拉基什的燃燒之劍「鑄魂」。

column1: You are exhausted from lack of sleep.
column2: 你因睡眠不足而精疲力竭。

column1: You are forever welcome, friend of the Katta.
column2: 你永遠受歡迎,卡塔族的朋友。

column1: You are getting tired.
column2: 你開始覺得累了。

column1: You are most welcome,[  Hero who came here.[May you always be so[  Welcome in Shapeir.
column2: 歡迎你,[  遠道而來的英雄。[願你永遠如此[  受夏皮爾歡迎。

column1: You are most welcome, O Hero.
column2: 不客氣,英雄。

column1: You are nearly overwhelmed by the amount of magic in the area.
column2: 這一帶的魔法之濃,幾乎讓你招架不住。

column1: You are not close enough.
column2: 你還不夠近。

column1: You are not permitted to ask questions in Raseir.
column2: 在拉希爾,你不准發問。

column1: You are not the official town crier. There's no reason to start yelling through the windows.
column2: 你又不是官方的街頭報事人,沒理由對著窗戶大喊大叫。

column1: You are now officially a monster mangling master!
column2: 你現在正式成了一名殘怪高手!

column1: You are now protected by the power of Reversal.
column2: 你現在受「反轉」之力護佑。

column1: You are really getting hungry.
column2: 你真的開始餓了。

column1: You are so exhausted everything hurts. Better get some rest.
column2: 你累得渾身痠痛。還是歇一歇吧。

column1: You are too good. I have already lost all the money I can afford to lose.
column2: 你太厲害了。我輸得起的錢,已經全輸光了。

column1: You ask Shameen and Shema about how and why they came to Spielburg.
column2: 你問起沙明和雪瑪,他們是如何、又為何來到史畢柏格的。

column1: You ask Shameen and Shema about their journey to Spielburg.
column2: 你問起沙明和雪瑪前往史畢柏格的旅程。

column1: You ask Shameen and Shema about their stay in Spielburg.
column2: 你問起沙明和雪瑪在史畢柏格的那段日子。

column1: You awaken as the sun begins to rise.
column2: 你在朝陽初升時醒來。

column1: You awaken as the sun sets behind the mountains.
column2: 你在夕陽沉入山後時醒來。

column1: You awaken at dawn after a fitful sleep full of disturbing dreams.
column2: 歷經一夜惡夢連連、輾轉難眠,你在破曉時醒來。

column1: You awaken at nightfall after a fitful sleep full of disturbing dreams.
column2: 歷經一夜惡夢連連、輾轉難眠,你在入夜時醒來。

column1: You awaken much refreshed as the sun begins to rise.
column2: 你在朝陽初升時醒來,精神煥然一新。

column1: You awaken much refreshed, as the sun sets behind the mountains.
column2: 你在夕陽沉入山後時醒來,精神煥然一新。

column1: You better find some food *soon*. you're starving!
column2: 你最好*快點*找些吃的。你快餓壞了!

column1: You breathily describe the Air Elemental to Harik.
column2: 你氣喘吁吁地向哈里克描述風元素精靈。

column1: You call out to nobody in particular.
column2: 你漫無對象地喊了一聲。

column1: You can barely even walk while carrying this much excess weight, let alone fight.
column2: 背著這麼多多餘的重量,你連走路都勉強,更別說打鬥了。

column1: You can faintly hear what sounds like somebody snoring.
column2: 你隱約聽見像是有人打鼾的聲音。

column1: You can hardly walk carrying so much weight, let alone climb into the saddle.
column2: 背著這麼重,你連走路都費勁,更別說爬上馬鞍了。

column1: You can hear a demented tapping on the door's far side.
column2: 你聽見門那頭傳來一陣瘋狂的敲擊聲。

column2: 你聽見這門後傳來一陣微弱的魔法旋繞聲。

column2: 你聽得見白蟻啃咬的聲音。

column1: You can make out the mountains beyond the buildings to the north.
column2: 你能辨認出北面建築之外的群山。

column2: 走遍整個市集,你也找不到比這更好的美味。

column1: You cannot manage to Fetch the feather with such a large rock weighing it down.
column2: 有這麼一塊大石頭壓著,你沒法把羽毛「取」過來。

column1: You cannot reach your Saurus from such a distance.
column2: 隔這麼遠,你構不著你的索魯斯。

column1: You can now climb with the best of them!
column2: 你現在攀爬的本事,已能與高手比肩!

column2: 你能看見那邊棚廄裡的坐騎索魯斯。

column2: 你不准再進來。我們才剛把你轟出去!

column1: You can't count the money at the moment!
column2: 你此刻沒法數錢!

column1: You can't Dazzle the creature if it isn't facing you.
column2: 那生物若沒面對著你,你就沒法對牠施「炫目」。

column1: You are carrying a basket filled with sand from the desert.
column2: 你帶著一個裝滿沙漠沙子的籃子。

column1: We should all die of thirst!
column2: 我們都會渴死的!

column1: Rakeesh looks slightly healthier than yesterday and a lot more at ease now that the city is no longer shaking.
column2: 拉基什看來比昨天硬朗些了,城市不再震動後,他也自在了許多。

column1: Shameen and Shema watch you and exchange a shocked glance. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 沙明和雪瑪看著你,交換了一記驚愕的眼神。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: Shapeir is such an interesting land, my good friend! I, of course, am your best source for local information.
column2: 夏皮爾真是個有意思的地方,我的好朋友!當然啦,要打聽本地消息,我就是你最好的來源。

column1: That strange man bears a striking resemblance to some of the other odd characters you've met around this city.
column2: 那個怪人,跟你在這城裡遇過的幾個古怪傢伙長得出奇地像。

column1: The Liontaur's fierce appearance contrasts greatly with the innocence of the infant sharing the mat with him.
column2: 那獅人兇猛的外表,與同蓆而坐的嬰孩那份天真,形成強烈的對比。

column1: This incense is made from the finest frankincense. It should attract the flame as the moth is attracted to it.
column2: 這薰香用最上等的乳香製成,該能像火吸引飛蛾那般,把火焰引來。

column1: When you listen very carefully at the door, it rumbles from deep within and says, "I am the Door of Destiny."
column2: 你十分仔細地貼門細聽,只聽它從深處隆隆作響,說道:「我乃命運之門。」

column2: 你此刻沒法那麼做。

column2: 你早料到那鐵匠不會樂意跟人友好地握手。

column2: 你沒法在那兒用那件物品。

column2: 想搬動那道沉重的鐵柵,你連指望都別指望。

column2: 你在樹下蜷起身子小睡片刻。

column2: 還是別了。那是你最後一把匕首,戰鬥時或許用得上。

column2: 你最好想個法子掙脫那些鐐銬……動作快點!

column2: 你決定不出聲。在這兒,你可不想招來多餘的注意。

column2: 你不動聲色地把那些字條撕碎,再把碎屑吃了下去。

column2: 你沒有魔法能力。

column2: 你收起了那條蠍尾。反正那東西擱在包裡,你也不怎麼安心。

column1: You put the small pot inside the chest, assured it'll be safer here than in your backpack.
column2: 你把小罐放進箱子,深信它擱在這兒總比擱在背包裡安全。

column1: You put the stored coins back into your purse.
column2: 你把存放的錢幣放回錢袋。

column2: 你收起了那個垃圾桶。

column1: You put your ear up to the door, but it ain't talkin'.
column2: 你把耳朵貼上門,可它一聲不吭。

column1: You quickly shake your head no. The voices' reaction was a bit too hostile for comfort.
column2: 你連忙搖頭否認。那些聲音的反應,敵意濃得令人不安。

column1: Your belongings go up in a puff of smoke.
column2: 你的家當化作一縷青煙,消失無蹤。

column1: Your breath alone could blow this door down.
column2: 光憑你一口氣,就能把這扇門吹倒。

column1: Your chainmail armor is quite effective for protection, but it is VERY heavy.
column2: 你的鎖子甲防護效果相當好,但「非常」沉重。

column1: Your compass doesn't seem to work in this place.
column2: 你的羅盤在這地方似乎失靈了。

column1: Your compass never tells you which way to go, but it always tells you which way you're facing.
column2: 你的羅盤從不告訴你該往哪走,卻總能告訴你正面朝著哪。

column1: You're about as stealthy as the average Goon.
column2: 你潛行起來,跟一般的打手一樣藏不住身。

column1: You reach out to the sky.
column2: 你朝天空伸出手。

column1: You read the first note you received.
column2: 你讀起收到的第一張字條。

column1: You read the note you received.
column2: 你讀起收到的那張字條。

column1: You read the second note you received.
column2: 你讀起收到的第二張字條。

column1: You read the third note you received.
column2: 你讀起收到的第三張字條。

column1: You realize you don't have even a full dinar to give.
column2: 你發覺自己連一整枚第納爾都拿不出來給。

column1: You really don't feel like resting right now.
column2: 你現在真的不想休息。

column1: You're already climbing it!
column2: 你已經在爬了!

column1: Your ears are deafened by rocks crashing and shattering against one another.
column2: 岩石互相撞擊碎裂的巨響,震得你耳朵發聾。

column1: You're barred from doing that.
column2: 你被禁止那麼做。

column1: You're currently riding your trusty ship of the desert.
column2: 你正騎著你那艘可靠的「沙漠之舟」。

column1: You relate the tale of how made the Earth Elemental bite the dust.
column2: 你講起自己如何讓土元素精靈一敗塗地的故事。

column1: You relate the tale to your friends about how you made it through the Brigands' lair.
column2: 你向朋友們講起,你是如何闖過強盜巢穴的。

column1: You remember being told never to shake hands with a Gnome without wearing rubber gloves.
column2: 你記得有人叮囑過:沒戴橡膠手套,千萬別跟地精握手。

column1: You're more likely to make it out alive if you don't announce your presence to the guard.
column2: 你要是不向衛兵張揚自己在場,活著脫身的機會還大些。

column1: You're no longer overloaded. That's a load off your mind.
column2: 你不再超重了。這下心頭也卸了個擔子。

column1: You're not close enough.
column2: 你還不夠近。

column1: You're not in a good position to do that.
column2: 你的位置不適合那麼做。

column1: You're not interested in any of the bottles right now.
column2: 你現在對那些瓶子都不感興趣。

column1: You're not interested in any skin moisturizer right now.
column2: 你現在對任何潤膚乳都不感興趣。

column1: You're not on as-sign-ment.
column2: 你又沒在執行什麼「招牌」任務。

column1: You're not supposed to cast spells on yourself.
column2: 你不該對自己施法術。

column1: You're not wearing them.
column2: 你沒穿著它們。

column1: You're really stuffed already. You decide not to take advantage of your friends' hospitality.
column2: 你已經撐得不行了。你決定不再叨擾朋友的款待。

column1: You're supposed to save the city with this stuff, remember?
column2: 你該用這東西去拯救這座城,記得吧?

column1: You're too far away to offer the Saurus anything.
column2: 你離得太遠,沒法餵那索魯斯任何東西。

column1: You retrieve a healing pill.
column2: 你取回一顆治療藥丸。

column1: You retrieve all of your items from the storage chest.
column2: 你把所有物品從儲物箱取了回來。

column2: 你取回一顆法力藥丸。

column2: 你取回一顆解毒藥丸。

column2: 你取回一份口糧。

column2: 你取回一顆活力藥丸。

column2: 你取回那袋沙子。

column1: You retrieve the basket of sand.
column2: 你取回那籃沙子。

column1: You retrieve the bellows from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那只風箱。

column2: 你從儲物箱取回那隻藍青蛙。

column1: You retrieve the bouquet.
column2: 你取回那束花。

column1: You retrieve the brass jar.
column2: 你取回那只黃銅罐。

column1: You retrieve the brass lamp.
column2: 你取回那盞銅燈。

column1: You retrieve the cloth bag.
column2: 你取回那個布袋。

column1: You retrieve the compass from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那個羅盤。

column1: You retrieve the container of oil.
column2: 你取回那罐油。

column1: You retrieve the decorative pot.
column2: 你取回那只裝飾用的陶罐。

column1: You retrieve the Dervish beard from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那撮苦行僧鬍鬚。

column1: You retrieve the dispel potion.
column2: 你取回那瓶驅散藥水。

column1: You retrieve the Djinni ring from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那只鎮尼戒指。

column1: You retrieve the emerald bowl from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那只翡翠碗。

column1: You retrieve the empty pot.
column2: 你取回那只空罐。

column1: You retrieve the fan.
column2: 你取回那把扇子。

column1: Isn't it exciting? Of course, some people don't appreciate its beauty. They actually want to get rid of it!
column2: 夠刺激吧?當然啦,有些人不懂得欣賞它的美,還真想把它弄掉呢!

column1: Keep going until you go north for two times. The next time you go north, her house is the first door on the west.
column2: 一直走,往北走兩次。下次再往北時,她家就是西邊第一扇門。

column1: That organization is supposed to be secret. Surely you don't want to go around telling everybody about it, do you?
column2: 那組織照理說是祕密的。你總不至於想到處跟人張揚吧?

column1: That was a good fight, but I still can't believe you didn't finish the job. The best members of EOF fight to kill.
column2: 這架打得不錯,可我還是不敢相信你竟沒了結對手。永恆戰士團最頂尖的成員,出手就是要取命。

column1: The Apothecary may be able to help you with your affliction, Effendi. He can be found in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 閣下,藥師或許能治您的病痛。他人在噴泉廣場。

column1: The Flame Dart gets extinguished as soon as it touches the Water Elemental. Perhaps another approach is in order.
column2: 火鏢一碰到水元素精靈就熄了。或許該換個法子。

column1: The lack of a shopkeeper and available merchandise seriously hampers your ability to do any shopping at this stall.
column2: 這攤子既沒店主、也沒貨,你在這兒實在沒法購物。

column1: The Saurus is nowhere to be seen. Perhaps using your steed for target practice wasn't such a great idea after all.
column2: 那索魯斯不見蹤影。看來拿自己的坐騎當靶子練習,終究不是什麼好主意。

column1: The shape of the lamp makes it impossible to scoop the Fire Elemental up. You'll have to think of something else.
column2: 這燈的形狀沒法把火元素精靈舀起來。你得另想辦法。

column1: The weaponry might be suitable for city guards, but you can tell the average quality would hamper monster-slaying.
column2: 這些兵器給城衛兵用或許還行,但你看得出,這平庸的品質拿去殺怪可會拖後腿。

column1: This broken pot must have housed a plant once. The dust on the bottom of the pot suggests that was a long time ago.
column2: 這只破罐子想必曾種過植物。罐底的積灰顯示那已是很久以前的事了。

column1: We should really get to know each other better. I am so eager to find out if your actions reflect your sweet words.
column2: 我們真該多認識認識彼此。我好想知道,你的行動是否配得上你那些甜言蜜語。

column1: You certainly know how to drive a hard bargain. Why don't we just agree at thirty for bridle, saddle, and Saurus?
column2: 你殺價可真有一套。不如就說定三十,馬轡、馬鞍連索魯斯全包了,如何?

column1: You collapse in the hot desert air and never awaken. Truly it is said that "water is life itself in a desert land."
column2: 你在炙熱的沙漠空氣中倒下,再也沒有醒來。古人說得真對:「在沙漠之地,水就是生命本身。」

column1: You don't want to miss the caravan in the morning, so you return to your room at the inn to prepare for the trip.
column2: 你不想錯過早上的商隊,於是回到客棧房裡,為這趟路做準備。

column1: You retrieve the fine sword from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那把好劍。

column1: You retrieve the flat box from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那只扁盒子。

column1: You retrieve the flower art.
column2: 你取回那件花藝。

column1: You retrieve the Fruit of Compassion from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回慈悲之果。

column1: You retrieve the game box.
column2: 你取回那個遊戲盒。

column1: You retrieve the genuine Ali Fakir X-Ray Glasses from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那副阿里·法基爾正牌透視眼鏡。

column1: You retrieve the Ghoul claw.
column2: 你取回那只食屍鬼爪。

column1: You retrieve the Ghoul's head.
column2: 你取回那顆食屍鬼的頭。

column1: You retrieve the Griffin feather from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那根獅鷲羽毛。

column1: You retrieve the incense.
column2: 你取回那份薰香。

column1: You retrieve the inn key from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那把客棧鑰匙。

column1: You retrieve the jade bracelet from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那只玉鐲。

column1: You retrieve the leather jerkin from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那件皮背心。

column1: You retrieve the lock pick from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那把開鎖器。

column1: You retrieve the magic earth from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那份魔法泥土。

column1: You retrieve the magic lamp from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那盞魔法神燈。

column1: You retrieve the magic rope from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那條魔繩。

column1: You retrieve the map from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那張地圖。

column1: You retrieve the notes.
column2: 你取回那些字條。

column1: You retrieve the ornate scimitars from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那些華麗的彎刀。

column1: You retrieve the poisonous Scorpion tail.
column2: 你取回那條有毒的蠍尾。

column1: You retrieve the pot of dirt.
column2: 你取回那罐泥土。

column1: You retrieve the powder of burning.
column2: 你取回那份燃燒之粉。

column1: You retrieve the protective boots from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那雙防護靴。

column1: You retrieve the purse from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那個錢袋。

column1: You retrieve the sand dome.
column2: 你取回那個沙暴罩。

column1: You retrieve the sapphire pin from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那枚藍寶石別針。

column1: You retrieve the silver dagger.
column2: 你取回那把銀匕首。

column1: You retrieve the sticker.
column2: 你取回那張貼紙。

column1: You retrieve the sword from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那把劍。

column1: You retrieve the tea service from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那套茶具。

column1: You retrieve the thieves' license from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那張盜賊執照。

column1: You retrieve the thieves' toolkit from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那套盜賊工具。

column1: You retrieve the trash can.
column2: 你取回那個垃圾桶。

column1: I haven't seen anything this bizarre since that guy from Iceland came along and offered to do a comedy routine.
column2: 自從那個從冰島來的傢伙提議表演段相聲後,我就沒見過這麼離奇的事了。

column1: I'm happy to hear that the dispel potion did its job after what you've been through to gather the ingredients.
column2: 聽說那瓶驅散藥水起了作用,我很高興——畢竟你為了湊齊材料,可吃了不少苦。

column1: I regret that there is no time for tales, Hero. You must finish your preparations before the caravan leaves you behind.
column2: 抱歉,英雄,沒空講故事了。你得在商隊把你丟下之前做好準備。

column1: It would be best if a person would sit down, that the Poet Omar might read and illuminate the minds of all who hear.
column2: 各位最好坐下來,讓詩人奧瑪爾吟誦,以啟迪所有聆聽者的心靈。

column1: I will be happy to listen to your stories when you return, Hero-friend. But I wish not to waste any of your time now.
column2: 你回來時,我很樂意聽你的故事,英雄朋友。但此刻我不願浪費你半點時間。

column1: Magic, magic, where'd it begin? Go and ask the mighty Djinn.[Magic, magic, where'd it stop? Look around my Magic Shop.
column2: 魔法魔法,從何而起?去問那威猛的鎮尼。[魔法魔法,止於何地?且看我這魔法店裡。

column1: My graduation present for you is the 'Reversal' Spell. You may recall the Kobold's use of it against you in Spielburg.
column2: 我給你的畢業禮物是「反轉」術。你或許還記得在史畢柏格,那狗頭人就是用它對付你的。

column1: Perhaps it's your proximity, or perhaps your choice of words, but in any event, you seem to have startled the Griffin.
column2: 也許是你靠得太近,也許是你的措辭,總之你似乎驚動了那頭獅鷲。

column1: Quest for Glory and all related material are copyrighted by Sierra Entertainment, Inc. and are used with permission.
column2: 《英雄傳奇》及一切相關素材的版權屬於 Sierra Entertainment, Inc.,經授權使用。

column1: Rumor has it that the captain of the Raseirian Guard is a master at swordplay. If you go there, you gotta fight him!
column2: 傳聞拉希爾衛隊長是個劍術高手。你要是去那兒,非跟他打一場不可!

column1: Saying goodbye to all our friends and relatives was difficult, but I was pleased to see how supportive everyone was.
column2: 向所有親友道別著實不易,但見大家如此鼎力相挺,我很欣慰。

column1: Seeing as no one in his right mind would actually want a Saurus, you won't mind that I have the right Saurus for you.
column2: 鑒於但凡神智正常的人都不會真想要一頭索魯斯,你應該不介意我這兒正好有頭合適的索魯斯吧。

column1: That really hurt. But it only really hurt for a few moments. The pain is quite gone now. So is every other sensation.
column2: 那真的很痛。不過也只痛了片刻。如今痛楚已盡數消退——其他一切知覺也是。

column1: The hope of the Katta was now resting on a prophecy that foretold the restoration of the city at the hands of a hero.
column2: 卡塔族的希望,如今寄託在一則預言上——它預示這座城將由一位英雄之手重獲新生。

column1: The note inside indicates that purse's owner enjoys poetry. Do you happen to know of one whom fits that description?
column2: 裡頭的字條顯示,這錢袋的主人喜愛詩歌。你可知道有誰符合這描述?

column1: The only proper way to dispose of your Saurus is to sell it. Simply disposing of the registration won't do the trick.
column2: 處理你索魯斯的唯一正當方式就是把牠賣掉。光把登記文件扔了可不管用。

column1: The powder of burning shimmers and sparkles as it shifts in the bag. You quickly close it again so as not to lose any.
column2: 燃燒之粉在袋中晃動時閃爍生輝。你連忙重新封好,免得漏掉半點。

column1: This pillar is too close to the wall and you need more room to Levitate. Try moving up to a different pillar instead.
column2: 這根柱子離牆太近,你「飄浮」需要更大空間。換到另一根柱子旁試試。

column1: Velvet curtains and marble casements signify that you could very well be situated in the more ritzy area of the city.
column2: 絲絨窗簾與大理石窗框,顯示你八成置身於城裡較為奢華的地段。

column1: What a shame you couldn't make it back to the cage in time. Now we'll never know whether the beast was enchanted or not.
column2: 真可惜你沒能及時趕回籠邊。如今我們永遠無從得知那野獸究竟有沒有中魔法了。

column1: You can't expect the Elemental to simply jump into your waterskin on its own. You'll have to think of something else.
column2: 你總不能指望那元素精靈自個兒跳進你的水袋吧。你得另想辦法。

column1: You decide to do your bit for world conservation and remove the box from the street. You take it into your possession.
column2: 你決定為環保盡份心力,把那盒子從街上撿走,據為己有。

column1: You drink the last few drops from your waterskin. You might want to start looking for a place where you can refill it.
column2: 你喝光了水袋裡最後幾滴水。你或許該開始找個能裝水的地方了。

column1: You no longer have your old sword. Getting rid of your current sword as well would make you a pretty pathetic fighter.
column2: 你的舊劍已經沒了。要是連現在這把也扔了,你這戰士可就太可悲了。

column1: You retrieve the undead unguent from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那瓶驅亡靈藥膏。

column1: You retrieve the Warrior's Diary.
column2: 你取回那本「戰士日記」。

column1: You retrieve the waterskin containing the essence of the Water Elemental from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那個裝著水元素精靈精華的水袋。

column1: You retrieve the weaving.
column2: 你取回那件編織物。

column1: You retrieve the wind in the bellows from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那只封著風的風箱。

column1: You retrieve your dagger from the carnage and carefully wipe it clean for reuse.
column2: 你從一片血肉狼藉中取回匕首,仔細擦淨以備再用。

column1: You retrieve your dagger from the corpse and carefully wipe it clean for reuse.
column2: 你從屍體上取回匕首,仔細擦淨以備再用。

column1: You retrieve your daggers from the carnage and carefully wipe them clean for reuse.
column2: 你從一片血肉狼藉中取回那些匕首,仔細擦淨以備再用。

column1: You retrieve your daggers from the corpse and carefully wipe them clean for reuse.
column2: 你從屍體上取回那些匕首,仔細擦淨以備再用。

column1: You retrieve your shield.
column2: 你取回你的盾牌。

column1: You retrieve your spare clothes.
column2: 你取回你的備用衣裳。

column1: You return the key to Shameen and Shema and thank them from the bottom of your heart for all they've done for you.
column2: 你把鑰匙還給沙明和雪瑪,打從心底感謝他們為你做的一切。

column1: You're up to your compass in Elementals! You can't use your map now.
column2: 你被元素精靈纏得脫不開身!現在沒法用地圖。

column1: You're walking through one of the many twisty streets of the city.
column2: 你正穿行在這城裡眾多曲折街道中的一條。

column1: You're walking through one of the twisting streets of the city.
column2: 你正穿行在這城裡一條曲折的街道上。

column1: You're welcome, but please, I must make haste.
column2: 不客氣,但請見諒,我得趕路了。

column1: Your Fetch spell is blocked by the cabinet door.
column2: 你的「取物」術被櫃門擋住了。

column1: Your greeting goes unanswered.
column2: 你的問候無人回應。

column1: Your hand passes right through the Djinni. He is entirely made of magic, after all.
column2: 你的手直接穿過了那鎮尼。畢竟,他渾身都是由魔法構成的。

column1: Your hand would make a nice appetizer for him. You wisely decide to withdraw your offering.
column2: 你的手拿去當他的開胃菜倒挺合適。你明智地收回了伸出的手。

column1: Your heavy, one-handed broadsword is an admirable weapon for a strong warrior.
column2: 你那把沉重的單手闊劍,對強壯的戰士而言是件令人稱羨的兵器。

column1: Your magic rope performs well.
column2: 你的魔繩很好使。

column1: Your mind is a jumble of half-completed thoughts.
column2: 你腦中盡是一堆殘缺不全的念頭。

column1: Your pawn isn't a valid target for that spell.
column2: 你的棋子不能當那個法術的目標。

column1: Your registration papers are of no interest to the Saurus. They simply permit you to ride it.
column2: 你的登記文件那索魯斯一點興趣也沒有,它們不過是准你騎牠罷了。

column1: Your Reversal spell has worn off.
column2: 你的「反轉」術效力已過。

column1: Your Reversal spell is wearing off.
column2: 你的「反轉」術效力正在消退。

column1: You retrieve the chainmail armor from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子取回那件鎖子甲。

column1: Good day, my fine friend. I trust you are finding your stay in Shapeir interesting? There is always so much to see and do here.
column2: 你好,我的好朋友。想必你在夏皮爾過得挺有意思吧?這兒永遠有看不完、玩不盡的東西。

column1: Hey, the Fire Elemental burn down my shop. I thinka maybe I gotta go into fire insurance next. If I insure you, you get fired.
column2: 嘿,火元素精靈把我店給燒了。我想啊,下回也許該改賣火災保險。我要是承保你,你就會被「開除」(著火)。

column1: Holes have been burned into the soles of your boots. They won't be of any use to you anymore unless you have them mended.
column2: 你的靴底被燒出了破洞。除非拿去補,否則它們對你再也沒用了。

column1: I am honored by your challenge, but I would like to see you develop your combat skills further before accepting it.
column2: 你的挑戰是我的榮幸,但在接受之前,我想先看你把戰鬥本事再練精些。

column1: I am sorry, but I know little of the art of fighting with a dagger and thus wouldn't be an appropriate sparring partner for you.
column2: 抱歉,我對匕首格鬥所知甚少,當不了你合適的切磋對象。

column1: I am sorry, but I used the last of my ingredients making the last one, so I will be unable to make you another.
column2: 抱歉,做上一個時材料就用光了,我沒法再給你做一個。

column1: I carry specially made beggar purses with hidden pockets, designed to look empty even when there is money contained within.
column2: 我賣的是特製的乞丐錢袋,帶暗袋,就算裡頭裝著錢,看上去也像是空的。

column1: If the creature could talk, it would likely not ask questions before attacking. And you know what that means, don't you?
column2: 那生物若會說話,八成攻擊前也不會多問。這話什麼意思,你懂吧?

column1: If you sold the silver dagger right now, you'd be completely unarmed. You're better off holding on to it for the time being.
column2: 你現在要是把銀匕首賣了,可就赤手空拳了。暫時還是留著為妙。

column1: If you want to show your affiliation with the profession, you should be more subtle about it and use the thief sign instead.
column2: 你若想表明自己是這行的,該含蓄些,改用盜賊暗號才對。

column1: If you would but purchase one of my lamps, Effendi, you may discover a Djinni inside who can cure you of that particular problem.
column2: 閣下,您只要買下我一盞燈,說不定會發現裡頭有個鎮尼,能治您那毛病。

column1: I have heard a rumor that you are taking a caravan to Raseir. It would be best if you purchased some extra pills to take with you.
column2: 我聽聞你要搭商隊去拉希爾。你最好多買些藥丸隨身帶著。

column1: I have heard that Raseir has become a grim and unpleasant place to be, now that my good friend Arus is no longer the Emir.
column2: 我聽說自從我的好友阿魯斯不再是埃米爾,拉希爾就變成了個陰森難熬的地方。

column1: I have prepared a meal of rabbit with apricots and roast chicken with sherbet for this night's meal. May it bring you pleasure.
column2: 今晚的菜我備了杏桃燉兔肉,還有烤雞佐果露。願它讓您吃得開心。

column1: In the past, plants probably thrived beneath the window. Now, the only thing inside the plant case is a thick layer of dust.
column2: 從前窗下大概花草繁茂。如今,那花盆裡只剩厚厚一層灰。

column1: It is a shame I could not be around to see you defeat it. It would have been a sight to behold. Congratulations on your victory.
column2: 可惜我沒能在場看你擊敗它,那場面必定壯觀。恭喜你獲勝。

column1: It is good you have managed to dispel the curse of the ogress so the Baroness and the other deceased can know peace again.
column2: 你成功解除了女妖的詛咒,讓男爵夫人與其他亡者得以重歸安寧,真是太好了。

column1: It's a long way to tip a ratty? Or maybe, 'Cheese it, I swiss I hadn't gone this whey.' Oh, all right, what did the rat say?
column2: 「打賞一隻老鼠路途遙遠」?還是「快閃哪,我真『起司』(希望)沒走這條『乳清』路」?唉,好啦,那隻老鼠到底說了什麼?

column1: It would be best not to announce to your kind hosts that you are a thief. They might make you pay room and board after all.
column2: 最好別向善待你的主人們張揚自己是盜賊。否則他們搞不好真會跟你收食宿費。

column1: It would be in poor taste to announce to a man of such meager means that you are in the profession of stealing from people.
column2: 向一個如此清貧的人宣告你是靠偷竊維生的,實在不近人情。

column1: I will need the "Fruit of Compassion." You must add the hair of the victim yourself at the last moment. It must be fresh.
column2: 我需要「慈悲之果」。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻親手加進去,而且必須是新鮮的。

column1: Know this, though. I am the Thieves' Guild here. Do not even think of using your skills around here without consulting me first.
column2: 不過你給我聽好:這兒的盜賊公會就是我。沒先跟我打過招呼,你休想在這一帶動你那身本事。

column1: Nope, sorry, boss. All outta anti-itch cream today. Maybe you should leave-a my stand now so I don't-a catch whatta you have.
column2: 沒啦,抱歉囉,老大。今天止癢膏賣光了。你還是離我的攤子遠點吧,免得我染上你那毛病。

column1: Normal weapons not be harming Elemental, but it not block fire and be too slow to dodge. Elemental only knows how to attack.
column2: 普通兵器傷不了元素精靈,但牠擋不了火,閃避又太慢。元素精靈只懂得進攻。

column1: Omar glances at you, then quickly starts thumbing through his book of poems. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 奧瑪爾瞥了你一眼,旋即匆匆翻起他的詩集。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: Thank you, my friend. I have no idea where you managed to obtain my favourite food, but your gift is greatly appreciated.
column2: 謝謝你,我的朋友。我不知道你是打哪兒弄來我最愛吃的東西的,但這份禮物我感激不盡。

column1: The black desert Scorpion is one of the deadliest creatures upon the sands, with sharp claws and a poisonous stinger for a tail.
column2: 黑色沙漠蠍是沙地上最致命的生物之一,長著利螯,尾部還有一根毒刺。

column1: The decorative basket, like the rest of the city, is in poor shape. It looks ready to fall apart at the slightest breeze.
column2: 那只裝飾用的籃子跟這城裡其他地方一樣破敗,看著一陣微風就要散架。

column1: The enchantment on the beast is far too powerful for your Trigger spell to reverse. You will have to think of something else.
column2: 那野獸身上的魔咒太過強大,你的「觸發」術逆轉不了。你得另想辦法。

column1: The enchantment on the tree is far too strong to be removed by your trigger spell. You will have to think of something else.
column2: 那棵樹上的魔咒太過強大,你的「觸發」術解除不了。你得另想辦法。

column1: The Katta wish to thank you for your bravery and kindness. Please go visit Sashanan the jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace today.
column2: 卡塔族想感謝你的勇敢與善心。請今天去王宮廣場拜訪珠寶商薩沙南。

column1: The odor of decay overwhelms your senses as you look into the barrel. Whatever's in there has been inside for a very long time.
column2: 你往桶裡一看,一股腐臭撲鼻,沖得你頭暈。不管裡頭是什麼,都已經擱了很久很久。

column1: The prophecy may have become a bit garbled over the years, but the original went like this when Suleiman taught it to me:
column2: 這預言經年累月或許有些走樣,但當年蘇萊曼教我時,原話是這樣的:

column1: There is little I can do about such a thing except to help the merchants get away and try to keep the fire from spreading.
column2: 這種事我能做的不多,頂多幫商人們脫身,再設法不讓火勢蔓延。

column1: The Sultan's army went out to discover what happened to the Emir of Raseir. A great magic stopped them, and few returned.
column2: 蘇丹派軍隊去查拉希爾埃米爾的下落。一股強大的魔法擋住了他們,生還者寥寥無幾。

column1: The swirling mass of water doesn't respond, however it does seem to make a low moaning sound as it grows in power and size.
column2: 那團旋繞的水沒有回應,卻似乎在力量與體型壯大之際,發出一陣低沉的呻吟。

column1: The traditionally shaped window is tightly shut as if the residence wishes to close its eyes to the current state of the city.
column2: 那扇傳統樣式的窗緊閉著,彷彿這宅子想對城市的現狀閉上雙眼。

column1: The traditional window is square-shaped in size and has been built into the wall. You notice dust and mold gathering on the edges.
column2: 這扇傳統的方形窗嵌在牆裡。你注意到邊緣積著灰塵與黴斑。

column1: The withered tree provides no protection from the sun during the day at all, so you should sleep here until the morning instead.
column2: 那棵枯樹白天根本擋不了太陽,所以你還是在這兒睡到天亮為好。

column1: This is the Apothecary, where one can buy many wondrous pills and oils. I am Harik. If you need something, just let me know.
column2: 這裡是藥舖,各種神奇的藥丸與藥油都買得到。我是哈里克。有什麼需要,儘管說。

column1: This month it is exactly a year ago since the old Emir of Raseir vanished and his successor drove our people away from their home.
column2: 這個月,正好是拉希爾的老埃米爾失蹤、其繼任者把我族人趕離家園滿一年。

column1: This used to be a rather ritzy sign, but the owner, if still in business, apparently couldn't afford to maintain it anymore.
column2: 這招牌從前還挺氣派,但店主——若還在營業的話——顯然已無力維護了。

column1: Tonight I have prepared something especially for you so that you do not become too homesick. It is knockwurst and sauerkraut.
column2: 今晚我特地替你準備了點東西,免得你太想家。是德式香腸佐酸菜。

column1: Use the correct sword technique in the appropriate situation and you'll be able to overcome any opponent within your abilities.
column2: 在恰當的情境用對劍招,你就能擊敗任何在你能力範圍內的對手。

column1: Wait, I think I hear an answer lurking in the curtains. You can't let those answers lurk, you know. It's bad for their health.
column2: 等等,我好像聽見有個答案躲在簾子後頭。你可不能讓那些答案老躲著,知道嗎,那對它們健康不好。

column1: When you hold the waterskin up to your ear, you hear a low swirling sound as the elemental moves about inside the container.
column2: 你把水袋舉到耳邊,聽見那元素精靈在容器裡游動時發出的低沉旋繞聲。

column1: You don't want to open the sealed game box. Ever since Chainsaw Monday, these vintage games have become real collector's items.
column2: 你不想拆開那個密封的遊戲盒。自從「電鋸星期一」以來,這些老遊戲都成了搶手的收藏品。

column1: You'd probably be capable of poking a hole in it with your finger if you desired to do so. You have no such desire, however.
column2: 你要是想,大概用手指就能在它上頭戳個洞。不過你並沒這念頭。

column1: You go until you can see a way to go left and you go to the left. Thatsa right way. Follow the road and you no can miss it.
column2: 你一直走,看到能往左拐的地方就往左。沒錯,就那條路。沿著走,絕錯不了。

column1: You have selected the Combo interface. This interface combines the best of both Text Parser and Point & Click interfaces.
column2: 你選了「複合」介面。這個介面結合了文字指令與點選操作兩者的優點。

column1: You notice Mirak gestures to Kiram who responds with a quick affirmative nod. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 你注意到米拉克朝基拉姆比了個手勢,基拉姆飛快地點頭應了一下。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: You notice Toshur has her hand in front of her mouth as if to cover a gasp. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 你注意到托舒爾以手掩口,像是要遮住一聲驚呼。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: Your efforts managed to blow the Water Elemental out of the fountain temporarily, but it quickly returned when you stopped.
column2: 你一番努力,總算暫時把水元素精靈吹出了噴泉,可你一停,它又迅速回來了。

column1: Your rope is beginning to feel limp. Perhaps its magical power is limited.
column2: 你的繩子開始發軟。也許它的魔力是有限的。

column1: Your rope is so limp that you don't know if it will rise for you at all. Its magic has been drained.
column2: 你的繩子軟得不行,你不確定它還撐不撐得起來。它的魔力已被耗盡。

column1: Your Saurus is intent on fleeing the scene.
column2: 你的索魯斯一心想逃離現場。

column1: Your Saurus' natural homing instinct quickly sets you on the right path.
column2: 你索魯斯與生俱來的歸巢本能,迅速把你領上了正路。

column1: Your Saurus needs more room before you can dismount.
column2: 你的索魯斯得有更大空間,你才能下來。

column1: Do you think you can cheat Honest Ali Fakir? You have to get up early in the morning to do that, since I sleep in. I need my beauty sleep.
column2: 你以為騙得了老實人阿里·法基爾?想騙我,你得起個大早,因為我向來睡懶覺——我需要美容覺。

column1: Due to the holes and cracks between the wicker, the basket would not be a suitable container for the magical earth.
column2: 柳條間滿是孔洞與裂縫,這籃子拿來裝魔法泥土可不合適。

column1: Fighters tend to be rough, tough, and rowdy. Choose the fighter if you want to take the direct approach to your problems.
column2: 戰士往往粗獷、強悍又火爆。你若想用最直接的方式解決問題,就選戰士。

column1: Forgive me, but Shema would be most displeased if I sold you something which you could not easily carry and for which you have no real use.
column2: 請見諒,我若賣你一件你帶起來不便、又派不上用場的東西,雪瑪會很不高興。

column1: I cannot think of anything more disrespectful than to disturb the peace of the deceased. That ogress is certainly one spiteful being.
column2: 我想不出有什麼比攪擾亡者安寧更不敬的事了。那女妖當真是個記恨的東西。

column1: If the first page of the book is to be believed, the Brigand's name is "Sweeping Sir James". The name has somewhat of familiar ring to you.
column2: 若這本書第一頁可信,那強盜名叫「橫掃詹姆斯爵士」。這名字你聽著有點耳熟。

column1: If you are inclined to climb, a magic rope can give you hope. To make it rise, close your eyes, raise your arms high, and watch it fly.
column2: 你若有意攀爬,魔繩能給你指望。想讓它升起,閉上雙眼,高舉雙臂,看它騰空翱翔。

column1: If you enjoy this game, please try the original version by Sierra (www.sierra.com) and also the other games in the Quest for Glory series.
column2: 你若喜歡本遊戲,請試玩 Sierra 的原版(www.sierra.com),以及《英雄傳奇》系列的其他作品。

column1: If you want to talk of thieves, take the Moneychanger. I'd like to take her myself. She will certainly take you for everything you've got.
column2: 要說盜賊,那錢莊老闆娘才算一號。我自己都想「拿下」她。她可是會把你身上的一切都「拿」個精光。

column1: I have gold rings for sale that can be engraved with a personal message of your choice. The message appears when the metal is heated.
column2: 我賣的金戒指可以刻上你選的私密留言。金屬一加熱,字就會顯現。

column1: I normally carry adventurer's clothes, but I ran out a few weeks ago. I am expecting some clothes suitable for savannah travel soon.
column2: 我平常賣冒險者的衣裳,但幾週前賣光了。我正等著進一批適合草原遠行的衣服。

column1: It is said that the Baron's wife was buried there, and that Baba Yaga put a curse on the graveyard so that her spirit could no longer rest.
column2: 據說男爵夫人就葬在那兒,芭芭雅嘎還對那墓地下了咒,叫她的靈魂再也不得安息。

column1: It's quite unlikely you'll get the chance to collect the ingredients required to make another dispel potion. You decide not to drop it.
column2: 你大概沒什麼機會再湊齊調製驅散藥水的材料了。你決定別把它丟掉。

column1: I've had tough customers before, but most speak better gibberish. For example, do you understand the phrase, 'Do you want to buy a Saurus?'
column2: 難纏的客人我見多了,可多數人胡言亂語都比你說得溜些。比方說,你聽得懂「你想買頭索魯斯嗎」這句話嗎?

column1: I will take five dinars for the Saurus, and I'll even throw in Travel Assurance. You'll travel, I'll assure you. Now, shall we agree?
column2: 這索魯斯我收五第納爾,還外加「旅行保證」——你儘管上路,我向你保證。怎麼樣,成交吧?

column1: Let us have a drink to our homeland then. May it have many more adventures in store for you and much good fortune in store for all of us.
column2: 那就為我們的家鄉乾一杯吧。願它為你備下更多冒險,也為我們眾人備下滿滿的好運。

column1: My heart says that you are a friend, yet my mind fears you are a trap. Is there not some token you can show me to prove what you say?
column2: 我的心說你是朋友,我的腦卻怕你是個圈套。你可有什麼信物能拿給我,證明你所言不虛?

column1: My specialty is boots with spurs on the heels. They are perfect for when you wish to give your Saurus an extra boost of speed during races.
column2: 我的拿手貨是後跟帶馬刺的靴子。賽跑時想讓你的索魯斯再添一股衝勁,它們再合適不過。

column1: My, you are the brave and fortunate one! I am now almost completely stocked with scorpion venom. I will purchase one more scorpion tail.
column2: 哎呀,你還真是又勇敢又走運!我的蠍毒如今幾乎備齊了。我再收一條蠍尾。

column1: Not long after our caravan left the valley, heavy snowfall blocked the pass out of town, isolating Spielburg from the rest of the world.
column2: 我們的商隊離開山谷後不久,一場大雪封住了出城的隘口,把史畢柏格與外界隔絕了開來。

column1: Perhaps one day you'll visit a land where you may attack random city guards at will. But here in Shapeir, you can't -- so stop trying!
column2: 也許有朝一日你會去到一個能隨意攻擊城衛兵的地方。但在夏皮爾這兒,你不能——所以別再試了!

column1: Quest for Glory II offers multiple conversation and alleyway systems to the player. Please select your preferences before starting.
column2: 《英雄傳奇 II》為玩家提供了多種對話與巷弄系統。開始前請先選擇你的偏好。

column1: Rakeesh still looks tired from his ordeal against the Earth Elemental, but also relieved after hearing of its defeat at your hands.
column2: 拉基什對戰土元素精靈的折磨仍讓他面露疲態,但聽聞它敗在你手下後,他也鬆了口氣。

column1: Realizing I had not only trapped myself in that cold land but Shema as well made me despair for the first time since we left Shapeir.
column2: 意識到我不僅把自己、也把雪瑪困在那片寒冷之地,我自離開夏皮爾以來頭一次陷入絕望。
