column1: Something tells you that the Poet does not deal with thieves, nor would he be too pleased to hear that you are one. You think better of it.
column2: 你有種感覺,那詩人不跟盜賊打交道,聽說你是盜賊也不會高興。你打消了念頭。

column1: So you are a jackal among the goats, then. I am very fond of those of the profession. This could be the start of a beautiful relationship.
column2: 這麼說,你是群羊裡的一隻豺狼囉。我可喜歡幹這行的人了。這說不定是一段美好交情的開端。

column1: SPECIAL ACTIONS MENU[[In the special actions menu you will find several more icons: WALK, RUN, SNEAK, REST, STATISTICS, TIME OF DAY, and OK.
column2: 特殊動作選單[[在特殊動作選單裡,你會找到更多圖示:行走、奔跑、潛行、休息、屬性、時辰,以及「確定」。

column1: The Elemental's not going to fit in that little bag in its current form, but you certainly will if you insist on taking this approach.
column2: 那元素精靈以目前的形態塞不進那小袋子——但你要是硬要這麼幹,你倒是塞得進去。

column1: The incense is too fine to have any lasting effect on the Air Elemental. It would get scattered and blown aside by the powerful vortex.
column2: 這薰香太細,對風元素精靈起不了持久作用,會被那強勁的氣旋吹散颳開。

column1: The large iron grate is rusty and its bars are wrought. Nevertheless, it still accomplishes its job of blocking further passage to you.
column2: 那道大鐵柵生了鏽,鐵條是鍛打的。儘管如此,它擋住你去路的本分倒是盡得稱職。

column1: The only effect is a tingling sensation. But, if the Apothecary is to be trusted, you should be safe from the effects of poison for a while.
column2: 唯一的感覺是一陣刺麻。不過,若藥師的話可信,你接下來一陣子應該不怕中毒了。

column1: The powder is too fine to have any lasting effect on the Air Elemental. It would get scattered and blown aside by the powerful vortex.
column2: 這粉末太細,對風元素精靈起不了持久作用,會被那強勁的氣旋吹散颳開。

column1: The sand is too fine to have any lasting effect on the Air Elemental. It would get scattered and blown aside by the powerful vortex.
column2: 這沙子太細,對風元素精靈起不了持久作用,會被那強勁的氣旋吹散颳開。

column1: The SCI version of Quest for Glory II: Trial by Fire was created in 1990 by Sierra On-line. This remake was made in 2008 by AGD Interactive.
column2: 《英雄傳奇 II:烈火試煉》的 SCI 版本由 Sierra On-line 於 1990 年製作。本重製版由 AGD Interactive 於 2008 年完成。

column1: Uhura is still practicing her counter techniques. If you keep your guard up in a counter duel, you'll get through her defenses eventually.
column2: 烏胡拉還在練她的反擊技。在反擊對決中只要你守住架勢,終究能突破她的防禦。

column1: Until you're certain that every conflict in the world can be solved by negotiation, keeping your sword handy might not be such a bad idea.
column2: 在你確信世上一切紛爭都能靠談判化解之前,把劍帶在手邊或許並非壞事。

column1: We caught glimpses of him at your reward ceremony at Spielburg castle. Unfortunately, we did not get an opportunity to speak with him.
column2: 在史畢柏格城堡為你舉行的授獎典禮上,我們瞥見過他幾眼。可惜沒能有機會跟他說上話。

column1: While you'd relieve your body of a heavy load by dropping your chainmail armor, you'd also relieve it of an important means of protection.
column2: 把鎖子甲丟了,固然能替身子卸下沉重的負擔,卻也卸下了一道重要的防護。

column1: Work is a little hard to come by in Shapeir. After what you have already done here, you should lay low, if you'll pardon the expression.
column2: 在夏皮爾找活兒有點難。你既已在這兒幹了那些事,恕我直言,你該低調一陣子。

column1: You assume this vase once contained a decorative plant. In its current state, it appears incapable of housing anything other than mold.
column2: 你猜這花瓶從前養過一株觀賞植物。就它如今的模樣,除了黴菌大概什麼也養不活。

column1: You have only to give me a dinar, and if you manage to cross the rope from one end to the other, then you will gain five dinars more.
column2: 你只要給我一第納爾,要是能從繩子這頭走到那頭,就能再贏得五第納爾。

column1: You have selected the Point & Click interface. You'll be able to select conversation topics by clicking on the subject in dialogue trees.
column2: 你選了「點選」介面。你將能在對話樹中點擊主題來選擇談話內容。

column1: Your assault left Rocky in very tiny pieces. Good thing you won, or your remains may have been spread across the place in a similar fashion.
column2: 你這一通猛攻,把「岩岩」打成了一塊塊碎渣。幸好你贏了,否則四散一地的殘骸,說不定就是你了。

column1: Your character has been imported as a fighter. If you do not feel this is the correct profession, please indicate your preference below...
column2: 你的角色已匯入為戰士。你若覺得這職業不對,請於下方指明你的偏好……

column1: Your character has been imported as a thief. If you do not feel this is the correct profession, please indicate your preference below...
column2: 你的角色已匯入為盜賊。你若覺得這職業不對,請於下方指明你的偏好……

column1: Your puny spell is no match for the vast magical influence in this room. Your Calm spell is quickly absorbed by the Statue of Iblis.
column2: 你那點微末法術,敵不過這房裡龐大的魔法之力。你的「鎮定」術迅速被伊布利斯的雕像吸收殆盡。

column1: Your Saurus probably wouldn't appreciate having a bumper sticker attached to its rear end.
column2: 你的索魯斯大概不會樂意屁股後頭被貼上一張保險桿貼紙。

column1: Your Saurus seems to want a drink.
column2: 你的索魯斯似乎想喝水。

column1: Your Saurus shows no sign of interest in your possessions.
column2: 你的索魯斯對你的家當毫無興趣。

column1: Your shield is made of hardwood and iron. It is both strong and heavy.
column2: 你的盾牌以硬木與鐵製成,既堅固又沉重。

column1: Your shield will be quite safe. Let's hope the same will apply to you if you get into another fight.
column2: 你的盾牌會很安全。但願你下回再捲入打鬥時,自己也能這麼安然無恙。

column1: Your slightest breath would probably be enough to knock it off its hinges.
column2: 你只要輕輕吹一口氣,大概就足以把它從鉸鏈上掀下來。

column1: Your spell does nothing. It only works upon magically enchanted objects.
column2: 你的法術毫無作用。它只對施了魔咒的物體有效。

column1: Your style of cleaning things up is a little different.
column2: 你清理東西的方式有點與眾不同。

column1: Your Thieves' Guild license authorizes you to do business with thieves without getting killed by them.
column2: 你的盜賊公會執照,授權你跟盜賊做買賣而不會被他們宰了。

column1: You run your fingers through the earth in the case, but it seems the plants that used to grow in here have been uprooted completely.
column2: 你用手指撥弄盆裡的泥土,但從前長在這兒的植物,似乎已被連根拔得一乾二淨。

column1: Your valor in the face of the dreaded Ghouls is impressive. My supply of claws is almost complete, but I will yet purchase a few more.
column2: 你面對可怕的食屍鬼時那份英勇,著實令人佩服。我的爪子快收齊了,但還願意再收幾隻。

column1: I am called Sadik Isfahani.
column2: 我叫薩迪克·伊斯法罕尼。

column1: Abdulla glances at his tea as if he can't make up his mind whether to drink it or not. It's evident that the news of the Water Elemental is bothering him.
column2: 阿布杜拉瞥著他的茶,彷彿拿不定主意該不該喝。顯然,水元素精靈的消息正困擾著他。

column1: Abdulla happened to be close to Spielburg when the road to the valley became accessible again and expected to make a small fortune by being the first trader to arrive in the valley that year.
column2: 通往山谷的路重新開通時,阿布杜拉正好在史畢柏格附近,指望著當那年頭一個抵達谷地的商人,好賺上一小筆。

column1: Abdulla seems a bit absent-minded and rarely seems to taste his food. It looks like news of the Fire Elemental didn't exactly improve his appetite.
column2: 阿布杜拉似乎有些心不在焉,吃東西也少有滋味。看來火元素精靈的消息可沒讓他的胃口好到哪去。

column1: Abdulla was asked to sell trade items that the Katta merchants in Shapeir supplied him with. He was supposed to use the profits to help us continue our journey.
column2: 阿布杜拉受託代售夏皮爾卡塔族商人供給他的貨物,本該用所得的利潤助我們繼續上路。

column1: Abu takes off his glasses, polishes them, puts them on again and continues looking at you. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 阿布摘下眼鏡,擦了擦,又戴回去,繼續盯著你看。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: According to the Chief in Spielburg, thieves are resentful of people stealing without paying membership to the union. You decide not to find out how resentful.
column2: 據史畢柏格的酋長說,盜賊們最恨那些不繳公會會費就偷東西的人。你決定還是別去領教他們有多恨。

column1: After the remainder of the Sultan's army returned and it became clear the Sultan did not want to send more men to Raseir, Shema and I decided to leave Shapeir and take the first caravan heading north.
column2: 蘇丹殘餘的軍隊歸來、而蘇丹顯然不願再派人馬前往拉希爾之後,雪瑪和我決定離開夏皮爾,搭上第一支北行的商隊。

column1: After trying to make a deal for some twenty minutes, you realize that you really don't need the pin THAT badly.
column2: 討價還價了約莫二十分鐘後,你發覺自己其實沒「那麼」需要那枚別針。

column1: After years of study, you became a very knowledgeable wizard. However, on occasion, you find yourself wondering what happened to Abdulla, the Katta, and all your other friends in Shapeir.
column2: 經年累月的鑽研,你成了一位學識淵博的巫師。然而,你偶爾仍會想起阿布杜拉、卡塔族,以及你在夏皮爾所有其他朋友的下落。

column1: Ah, many greetings, Effendi. Is it not a beautiful day today? We are, indeed, fortunate that such days have no monetary value placed upon them, and can be enjoyed by all.
column2: 啊,萬般問候,閣下。今天天氣可真好,不是嗎?我們的確有幸,這樣的日子不必標上價錢,人人都能享受。

column1: Ah, when I first saw you, I said to myself, there is a man who knows how to play the game. I appreciate that in a man.
column2: 啊,第一眼見到你,我就對自己說:這是個懂得門道的人。我就欣賞這樣的男人。

column1: Ah, you are obviously a very busy man, as am I. Perhaps we can talk over drinks another time. I will not detain you longer. Wilmer, show our friend to the door.
column2: 啊,你顯然是個大忙人,我也一樣。或許改日我們能邊喝邊聊。我不再耽擱你了。威爾默,送我們的朋友出去。

column1: Alas, Effendi, money from the land of Spielburg is of no use to one such as I. You need to exchange it for the coin of our realm.
column2: 唉,閣下,史畢柏格那地方的錢對我這種人沒用。你得把它換成我們這兒的錢幣。

column1: All right, let's try it this way. I've got the Saurus for you, and you're looking for the words to say how much you want him. That's why you need the Saurus, of course.
column2: 好吧,咱們換個說法。我這兒有頭索魯斯正適合你,你呢,正搜腸刮肚想說自己有多想要牠。當然啦,這正是你需要這索魯斯的原因。

column1: Although you know that Harik would be most generous if offered a real Fire Elemental of his own, you decide that the creature in the lamp might prove invaluable to you at some later date.
column2: 你雖知道,若送哈里克一頭真正屬於他自己的火元素精靈,他必定慷慨相報,但你還是決定:燈裡那頭生物,日後或許對你有無可估量的用處。

column1: And do come fully equipped. No one of your profession should travel without the proper tool, and a little oil to make the insertion easier.
column2: 記得裝備齊全再來。幹你這行的,出門怎能不帶上趁手的工具,還有一點好讓它更易插入的油。

column1: Any attempt to unlatch this door using your card would undoubtedly fail, considering that it is securely locked and bolted from the inside.
column2: 想用你的卡片撥開這門閂,必定徒勞——畢竟它從裡頭牢牢上了鎖、還閂著。

column1: As soon as he moves, jump back, use magic, and close the gap again while your opponent is recovering. This might not grant you quick victories, but will not result in quick defeats either.
column2: 他一動,你就往後跳,施法,趁對手回神時再縮短距離。這未必能讓你速勝,卻也不會讓你速敗。

column1: As they crossed the desert, a storm of sand came up out of nowhere and decimated the troops. The Sultan did not think this was a coincidence.
column2: 他們橫越沙漠時,一場沙暴憑空颳起,把軍隊摧殘殆盡。蘇丹並不認為這是巧合。

column1: As you put your ear to the keyhole of the locked door, you hear a voice boom, "Halt, he would seek the treasure of the Aztecs!"
column2: 你把耳朵貼上那上鎖門的鑰匙孔,聽見一個聲音轟然響起:「站住,此人竟想尋阿茲特克的寶藏!」

column1: As you store your toolkit in the chest, you're reminded about the story of a guy who hid his safe keys in his own safe.
column2: 你把工具箱收進箱子時,想起一個傢伙把保險箱鑰匙藏進自己保險箱裡的故事。

column1: At nightfall tonight, go the door at the end of 'Saif Darb.' Obey, or you'll regret it.
column2: 「今晚入夜後,去『Saif Darb』盡頭那扇門。乖乖照辦,否則你會後悔。」

column1: At the noon of this night, you will take Sharki Darb to Rani Tarik. Then turn right into the alley. The first house on your right will be locked, but unbarred.
column2: 今夜子時,你走 Sharki Darb 到 Rani Tarik,再右轉進巷子。你右手邊第一間房會上著鎖,但沒閂。

column1: Aziza's beauty in no way masks her intensity, purposefulness, and the aura of power around her. It is good when in her presence to be on your best behavior.
column2: 阿姿莎的美貌絲毫掩不住她的熱烈、堅定,以及周身那股力量的氣場。在她面前,最好謹守你最得體的舉止。

column1: Before Simba was born, I be doing some hunting in desert. Fought many monsters. If you be wanting to know more about them, just say so.
column2: 辛巴出生前,我在沙漠裡打過獵,跟不少怪物交過手。你要是想多了解牠們,儘管說。

column1: Behind this door, you can faintly hear someone proclaiming "Thank God you're here!", followed by the sound of an audience applauding. How odd!
column2: 這門後,你隱約聽見有人高呼「謝天謝地你來了!」,接著是一陣觀眾的掌聲。真古怪!

column1: Being unable to see sky, stars, or sun in these caves, you have lost all perception of time. You feel disoriented and decide not to rest now.
column2: 在這些洞穴裡看不見天空、星辰或太陽,你對時間的感知已全然喪失。你頭暈目眩,決定現在先不休息。

column1: Be very careful with it. You need to remember to be as far from the target as you can, or you might be damaged by the reaction.
column2: 用它時務必小心。你得記得離目標越遠越好,否則可能被那反應波及。

column1: Cast Reversal and magical attacks directed at you will rebound to their caster. But take care -- it will not help against the indirect effects of other spells.
column2: 施展「反轉」,衝著你來的魔法攻擊便會彈回施法者身上。但要當心——它擋不住其他法術的間接效果。

column1: Changing the alleyway system while you're still inside will trap you in the depths of the source for the rest of eternity.
column2: 你人還在裡頭時改動巷弄系統,會把你永世困在源頭的深處。

column1: Come back tomorrow evening or so if you are interested in doing for me a little favor. It could be to our mutual benefit.
column2: 你若有意替我幫個小忙,明晚前後再來。這或許對你我都有好處。

column1: Considering the physical condition of the undead creature, you'd be surprised if it was still capable of speaking.
column2: 就那亡靈生物的身體狀況看來,它要是還能說話,你才會吃驚。

column1: Darkness will lead you into a tomb. You will walk in fire, earth, water, and air, through temptation, and at last into despair and desolation.
column2: 黑暗將領你步入墳塚。你將行經火、土、水、風,穿越誘惑,最終墮入絕望與荒蕪。

column1: Dispel potion? I do have a recipe for that one, but it takes some rare ingredients to make. Are you sure you really need one?
column2: 驅散藥水?那個方子我倒是有,只是得用些稀有材料才調得成。你確定真需要一瓶?

column1: Donations are much appreciated, however, this foreign coin is of no use to me. You must first exchange your gold coins to dinars.
column2: 施捨我感激不盡,但這外地的錢幣對我沒用。你得先把金幣換成第納爾。

column1: Don't you know an answer when I'm questioning it? Let's get down to business, so I can do a business on you. I'm selling you a Saurus, and you're trying to buy one, right?
column2: 我都把答案擺到你面前問了,你還認不出?咱們言歸正傳,好讓我跟你做筆「買賣」。我在賣你一頭索魯斯,你正想買一頭,對吧?

column1: Drains are positioned at various locations throughout the alleyways. The foul odor emanating from this one suggests the sewer system is in no better condition than the rest of the city.
column2: 巷弄各處都設有排水口。這一個飄出的惡臭顯示,這兒的下水道系統並不比城裡其他地方好到哪去。

column1: Early at night, go to Sahir Tarik, off Kudra Darb. Look for the first door on your left. I have heard the owner of the house has accumulated a lot of wealth.
column2: 入夜後,去 Kudra Darb 岔出的 Sahir Tarik,找你左手邊第一扇門。我聽說那宅子的主人積攢了不少財富。

column1: Even reaching the town must have been quite an adventure for you. The mountainous area near Spielburg is most dangerous if one does not travel in a guarded caravan.
column2: 光是抵達這座城,對你而言想必就是一場不小的冒險。史畢柏格附近的山區,若不隨有護衛的商隊同行,可是極其危險。

column1: For the potion to be effective, a strand of the enchanted victim's own hair must be dropped in at the last minute. However, this is up to you.
column2: 藥水要生效,得在最後一刻投入一根中咒受害者本人的頭髮。不過,這就由你決定了。

column1: Fortunately, my leg has recovered quickly enough to allow me the opportunity to test your combat skills. I accept your challenge gladly, my friend.
column2: 所幸我的腿復原得夠快,讓我有機會考考你的戰鬥本事。我樂意接受你的挑戰,朋友。

column1: For you, it will take a mere 15 dinars to loosen my lips.
column2: 看在你的份上,區區十五第納爾,我這嘴就鬆了。

column1: For you, it will take a mere 20 dinars to loosen my lips.
column2: 看在你的份上,區區二十第納爾,我這嘴就鬆了。

column1: Go thatta way until you can turn right. Go right until you come to a place where you can go right again. Turn left. Follow the road and you no can miss it.
column2: 往那邊走,走到能右轉為止。一直右轉,直到又有個能再右轉的地方。左轉。沿著路走,絕錯不了。

column1: By actions rash,[    a person might then find,[ That others take exceptions[    to his crime.[ And rudeness returned[    rudely thus remind,[ That person to return[    to things sublime.
column2: 魯莽行事者,[    終會驚覺,[ 他人對其罪愆[    心生不滿。[ 以無禮回敬無禮,[    便如此提點,[ 那人該回返[    崇高之境。

column1: Drains are positioned at various locations throughout the alleyways. You can't imagine it raining much in Shapeir, so you assume these are mainly for clothesline washing and such.
column2: 巷弄各處都設有排水口。你想像不出夏皮爾會常下雨,於是猜這些主要是供晾衣洗滌之類用的。

column1: Finding her in Shapeir's maze of streets may be difficult without a map of the city. The street names may only provide you a limited means of orientation. Allow me to explain their meanings to you.
column2: 沒有城市地圖,要在夏皮爾迷宮般的街巷裡找到她,只怕不易。街名能給你的方向指引也很有限。容我向你解釋它們的含義。

column1: From Harik, you have obtained a quantity of the powder of burning. You are carrying 1 bag.
column2: 你從哈里克那兒取得了一些燃燒之粉。你帶著 1 袋。

column1: From Harik, you have obtained a quantity of the powder of burning. You have 1 bag stored.
column2: 你從哈里克那兒取得了一些燃燒之粉。你存放了 1 袋。

column1: Go to the east and then go south when you must. Follow the road and you be finding her.
column2: 往東走,該往南時就往南。沿著路走,你就會找著她。

column1: Greetings, my good fellow. I'm Honest Ali Fakir and this is my short-term Shapeirian Saurus shop. If it's Saurus chops you're after, then I'm not your man. Go see a butcher.
column2: 你好啊,老兄。我是老實人阿里·法基爾,這是我在夏皮爾的短期索魯斯店。你要是想找索魯斯排,那我可幫不上忙,去找屠夫吧。

column1: Have you seen the Fire Elemental? It is magic of the most marvelous kind. So very beautiful, the dancing flames. If I but had such a creature at my command, the pills I would make!
column2: 你見過火元素精靈嗎?那是最奇妙的一種魔法。那舞動的火焰,美極了。我若能差遣這麼一頭生物,做得出的藥丸可不得了!

column1: He could not remove the enchantment that blocked Elsa's memories, so he remained in her service all those years despite the fact he was not a crook at heart.
column2: 他解不開封鎖艾莎記憶的那道魔咒,於是儘管本性並非歹徒,這些年仍一直留在她身邊效力。

column1: Hello, Master. You still have three wishes left.
column2: 您好,主人。您還剩三個願望。

column1: Here is a pin with a sapphire of exceeding clarity and color like that of your eyes, and a simple but elegant gold setting with the color like that of your hair. It would be a gift worthy of a Sultan.
column2: 這枚別針鑲著一顆無比清澈的藍寶石,色澤如你的雙眸;簡樸而雅致的金鑲座,則似你的髮色。這是配得上蘇丹的一份禮物。

column1: He seems like a good person, but what a horrible place to live. It is difficult for me to believe someone would live in a cold, dank cave of his own free will.
column2: 他似乎是個好人,可那住處實在糟糕。我很難相信有人會心甘情願住在又冷又潮的洞穴裡。

column1: Hey, boss. You a-come to the right-a place here! My name, he is Alichica and you find-a yourself at the finest souvenir stand in all-a Shapeir.
column2: 嘿,老大,你可來對地方囉!我的名字叫阿里奇卡,你眼前這可是全夏皮爾最棒的紀念品攤。

column1: His sons both serve in the guard and occasionally work night shifts. That's why the door is occasionally unbarred. You should be fine as long as you can avoid being around when they arrive or leave.
column2: 他兩個兒子都當衛兵,偶爾值夜班。這就是那門偶爾沒閂的緣故。只要你避開他們來去的時候,應該就沒事。

column1: His sons both serve in the guard and occasionally work night shifts. That's why the door is often unbarred. You should be fine as long as you can avoid being around when they arrive or leave.
column2: 他兩個兒子都當衛兵,偶爾值夜班。這就是那門常常沒閂的緣故。只要你避開他們來去的時候,應該就沒事。

column1: Holes have been burned into the soles of your boots. They won't be of any use to you anymore unless you have them patched up.
column2: 你的靴底被燒出了破洞。除非拿去補,否則它們對你再也沒用了。

column1: However, that could not surpass the anxiety I would feel if Shameen traveled alone, leaving me here worrying for him. So I decided to go with him.
column2: 可這份不安,仍比不上沙明獨自上路、把我留在這兒替他擔憂時的那份焦慮。於是我決定隨他同行。

column1: I already gave you the bellows. What more do you want?
column2: 我風箱已經給你了。還想怎樣?

column1: I am always out of Scorpion venom to make poison cure pills, and Ghoul claws for my experiments. I will pay 20 dinars for the tail of a Scorpion, and 15 dinars for the claws of a Ghoul.
column2: 我做解毒藥丸總缺蠍毒,做實驗又總缺食屍鬼爪。一條蠍尾,我出二十第納爾;一對食屍鬼爪,出十五第納爾。

column1: I am expecting some very fine armor within the coming weeks. If it pleases you, I can offer you a discount on kung fu dresses, duck helms, and tiger suits.
column2: 接下來幾週我會進一批上好的盔甲。你若中意,功夫袍、鴨盔和虎裝我都能給你打個折。

column1: I am in awe of your bravery that you have defeated so many of these fearful monsters, but I am presently overstocked with Scorpion venom, and I can purchase no more.
column2: 你擊敗了這麼多可怕的怪物,你的英勇令我敬佩,可我眼下蠍毒存貨太多,沒法再收了。

column1: I am sorry, my friend. Battling the Earth Elemental has severely weakened my leg. I am afraid I shall have to decline your challenge until I have recovered.
column2: 抱歉,朋友。與土元素精靈一戰,我的腿傷得不輕。恐怕在康復之前,我只得婉拒你的挑戰。

column1: I am sorry, my friend, but the Water Elemental prevents us from drawing any water from the fountain with which to make our tea. We must reserve what little tea we have left to serve with our meals.
column2: 抱歉,朋友,水元素精靈害我們沒法從噴泉取水沏茶。剩下那一點茶,我們得留著配餐用。

column1: If you can but walk from one end to the other, I will give you ten dinars.
column2: 你只要能從這頭走到那頭,我就給你十第納爾。

column1: If you know what's good for you, you will obey orders. We will leave them here. If you don't follow them, you'll be sorry. We'll be watching you.
column2: 「你要是識相,就乖乖聽令。指令我們留在這兒。你不照辦,有你好受的。我們會盯著你。」

column1: I have a new assignment for you, if you're interested in another... job. For a small price, I'm willing to give you a gentle bump in the right direction.
column2: 我有個新差事給你,如果你對另一樁……活兒有興趣的話。花點小錢,我願意輕輕推你一把,指條明路。

column1: I have a substance called powder of burning that might be of some use against an Earth Elemental. Since you put it that way, I guess I will just have to give the powder to you.
column2: 我有種叫燃燒之粉的東西,對付土元素精靈或許有點用。你既然這麼說了,看來我也只好把這粉給你了。

column1: I have heard that it was you that vanquished the Elemental of Air as well as of Flame. Perhaps you now need to know something about the Element of Earth.
column2: 我聽說風與火兩頭元素精靈都是你擊敗的。或許你現在該了解些關於土元素的事了。

column1: I hear you be plenty good and beat the Elemental. You be some hero, for sure. It was no little honor, being able to use Rakeesh's sword. I will be giving it back, if you will be giving it to me.
column2: 我聽說你本事了得,把那元素精靈給打敗了。你準是個英雄沒錯。能使拉基什的劍,可不是小小的榮耀。你把劍給我,我這就還回去。

column1: I hope he will mature somewhat before the rule of the valley is entrusted to his care. A wise ruler regards his subjects as equals, rather than looking down on them.
column2: 我但願在山谷的治理託付到他手上之前,他能稍稍成熟些。明君視子民為平等,而非居高臨下。

column1: Inside, you have stored the essence of the Pizza Elemental. You have 1 slice left.
column2: 裡頭裝著你封存的「披薩元素精靈」精華。你還剩 1 片。

column1: In Spielburg too, a prophecy foretold the coming of a hero, but it was not that which lead you there. It was a poster in your local Guild Hall in Willowsby -- the one that called for a hero in Spielburg.
column2: 在史畢柏格,同樣有一則預言預示英雄的到來,但領你前去的並非那預言,而是你家鄉柳鎮公會大廳裡的一張海報——那張為史畢柏格徵求英雄的海報。

column1: INVENTORY: Within this inventory screen are several options:[[ARROW: Selects an item[[LOOK: Displays a description of an item.[[DROP: Allows you to drop an item.[[OK: Returns to the game.
column2: 物品欄:這個物品欄畫面裡有幾個選項:[[箭頭:選取物品[[查看:顯示物品的描述。[[丟棄:讓你丟下物品。[[確定:返回遊戲。

column1: I recall that when you agreed to accompany us on our journey back to Shapeir, you were most baffled by our reaction. You stood there with an awkward smile while Shema and I almost leapt for joy.
column2: 我記得當你答應陪我們返回夏皮爾時,你對我們的反應一頭霧水。你站在那兒尷尬地笑著,雪瑪和我卻幾乎要欣喜得跳起來。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the lamp light.
column2: 別費勁跟那燈火搭話了。

column1: Don't trouble yourself with such actions.
column2: 你不必費這個事。

column1: A long thread of twine, spread with damp articles of clothing is spanned across an open outdoor gap for maximum exposure to the sunshine. Commonly known as a clothesline.
column2: 一條長麻繩,掛著一件件濕衣物,橫跨在戶外開闊處,好讓它們最大限度地曬到太陽。一般稱之為「曬衣繩」。

column1: A swordsman such as yourself should focus on sticking close to his opponent in order to avoid being caught off guard by long-distance attacks, occasionally backing off when your opponent mounts an offense.
column2: 像你這樣的劍士,該專注於緊貼對手,以免被遠距攻擊打個措手不及;對手發動攻勢時,則偶爾後撤。

column1: But maybe you gotta no kids? Then what you want is what I gotta right here -- a 'Shapeir in a bottle.' You shake it and you see Shapeir in a sandstorm. I sell it justa for you for only twice what it worth.
column2: 不過你也許沒小孩?那你想要的,就是我這兒的這個——「瓶中夏皮爾」。你一搖,就看見夏皮爾陷入沙暴。看在你的份上,我只賣你它身價的兩倍。

column1: I regret, Hero-friend, that we are unable to spare any more tea. We are short on water, and the Water Elemental prevents us from reaching the fountain.
column2: 抱歉,英雄朋友,我們勻不出更多茶了。我們缺水,而水元素精靈又害我們到不了噴泉。

column1: I sell shoes and boots that are light and fine. There is no footwear better suited for dashing back to the city entrance with a fierce monster on your tail.
column2: 我賣的鞋靴又輕又好。論身後追著兇猛怪物、拔腿衝回城門口,沒有比這更合腳的了。

column1: I still recall the day you first came wandering into our inn. I could tell by the look on your face that you had never seen a Katta before in your life.
column2: 我至今仍記得你頭一回晃進我們客棧的那天。看你那神情,我就知道你這輩子從沒見過卡塔族。

column1: It did strike me that while her deeds are known by so many, little is known about Erana herself. It is almost as if she were a benevolent spirit, rather than a living being who lived among us at one time.
column2: 我的確注意到,她的事蹟雖為眾人所知,人們對伊瑞娜本人卻所知甚少。她幾乎像是一縷仁慈的精魂,而非曾活在我們之中的血肉之軀。

column1: I tella you where to find the Guild Hall. You go outa the plaza here and take-a the street until you can turna left. Follow the road and you no can miss it.
column2: 我告訴你公會大廳在哪。你出了這座廣場,沿街走到能左轉為止。沿著路走,絕錯不了。

column1: I tella you where to find the Guild Hall. You go outa the plaza here and take-a the street until you can turna left. You go left until you can no go more. Then you go right.
column2: 我告訴你公會大廳在哪。你出了這座廣場,沿街走到能左轉為止。一直左轉,走到不能再走。然後往右。

column1: It is a pity that I do not have any of the potion made up. I have most of the ingredients, though. If you could find the ingredients I am missing, then I could make some dispel potions for you.
column2: 可惜我手頭沒有調好的藥水。不過材料我大半都有。你若能找來我缺的那些材料,我就能替你調幾瓶驅散藥水。

column1: It is difficult for me to imagine you started your adventures in Spielburg with nothing but the knowledge gained from a correspondence course to rely on.
column2: 我很難想像,你在史畢柏格展開冒險時,憑藉的竟只有從函授課程學來的那點知識。

column1: It is the finest suit of mail in all the realm. The Caravan Master has already paid for it.
column2: 這是全國最上乘的一副鎖子甲。商隊隊長已經替它付過錢了。

column1: It may be wise to keep it. Your trained reflexes may end up having you stick your bare hand into a monster's face the next time you meet one.
column2: 留著它或許是明智的。你那練出來的反射動作,搞不好下回遇上怪物時,會讓你赤手往牠臉上招呼。

column1: It's a crime to possess things like these and you know it. But considering how much money you paid to get it, it'd also be a crime to discard it.
column2: 持有這種東西是犯法的,你心知肚明。但想想你花了多少錢才弄到手,把它丟了也同樣是種「罪過」。

column1: It's a good tool, fool, but remember the rule: the magic can't last, and on your last cast it'll go fast, so don't waste it or you might find yourself hanging in air with nothing there.
column2: 這是個好工具,傻小子,但記住規矩:魔法撐不久,你最後一次施展時它去得快,所以別浪費,否則你可能發覺自己懸在半空、腳下空無一物。

column1: It seems the news of the Fire Elemental's capture has already reached Abdulla. He mumbles something between mouthfuls of food, which sounds like 'good job.'
column2: 火元素精靈被擒的消息,似乎已經傳到阿布杜拉耳裡。他邊大口吃著東西邊咕噥了一句,聽起來像是「幹得好」。

column1: It's said that talking to a plant speeds up its growth. You may want to test that theory when you come across a case that actually has a plant inside.
column2: 據說跟植物說話能加快它生長。等你哪天碰上一個裡頭真有植物的花盆,不妨驗證一下這說法。

column1: It's used to make dark stains on light clothes. If you need it to ground old Blowhard, it's yours for a song. Dust in the wind. All you need is dust in the wind.
column2: 它能在淺色衣物上染出深漬。你若需要它來收拾那老吹牛皮的,賤價就賣你。風中之塵啊,你需要的不過是風中一撮塵。

column1: It's your friend Abdulla Doo, whom you met at the inn in Spielburg after he was robbed by the Brigands. In return for recovering his treasure and his carpet, he brought you with him to Shapeir.
column2: 是你的朋友阿布杜拉·杜,你在史畢柏格客棧遇見他時,他剛被強盜洗劫過。為報你尋回他的財寶與地毯之恩,他帶你一同來到夏皮爾。

column1: It takes real muscle to be good at arm wrestling. I'm the best. If you want me to prove it, let me know and I'll be glad to wrestle you.
column2: 比腕力要比得好,得有真本事的肌肉。我就是最強的。你想要我證明,儘管說,我很樂意跟你扳一扳。

column1: It was a mean, nasty, agitated, about-to-rip-your-head-off Griffin. Now it's merely about to be supper for those vultures who have no moral issues with semi-cannibalism.
column2: 那本是頭兇惡、暴躁、就要把你腦袋扯下來的獅鷲。如今,牠不過是那群對「半同類相食」毫無道德顧忌的禿鷲的一頓晚餐罷了。

column1: It will probably take all the powder I can give you to defeat such a thing as the Earth Elemental. Do not waste it, and please do not miss when you throw it.
column2: 要擊敗土元素精靈這種東西,大概得用上我能給你的全部粉末。別浪費,擲的時候也千萬別失手。

column1: It will probably take most of the powder I can give you to defeat such a thing as the Earth Elemental. Do not waste it, and please do not miss when you throw it.
column2: 要擊敗土元素精靈這種東西,大概得用上我能給你的大半粉末。別浪費,擲的時候也千萬別失手。

column1: It would be very possible to lure the Fire Elemental away from the plazas using this incense, and where it is weak from hunger and water, capture it.
column2: 用這薰香把火元素精靈引離廣場,再趁牠因飢渴而虛弱時將牠捕獲,大有可為。

column1: I will need a Griffin's feather and the "Fruit of Compassion." You must add the hair of the victim yourself at the last moment. It must be fresh.
column2: 我需要一根獅鷲羽毛和「慈悲之果」。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻親手加進去,而且必須是新鮮的。

column1: Just to show you what a swell person I am, I'll throw in a free saddle and bridle with the purchase. In fact, I'll even let you have free room and board at the stable.
column2: 為了讓你見識見識我這人多夠意思,買了我還免費附送馬鞍和馬轡。事實上,連在馬廄的食宿我都讓你白住白吃。

column1: Katta communities are very close-knit. We were positive that, if the letter arrived safely, our people would do everything in their power to help us.
column2: 卡塔族的社群極其緊密。我們深信,只要那封信平安送達,族人必定竭盡所能來幫我們。

column1: KEYS AND INSTRUCTIONS.[[F1: Displays this help screen.[[F5: Save menu.[[F6: Cast last non-targetted spell.[[F7: Restore menu.[[F9: Restart the game.[[F12: Save screenshot.[[CTRL-Q: Quit the game.
column2: 按鍵與說明。[[F1:顯示此說明畫面。[[F5:存檔選單。[[F6:施放上一個無目標法術。[[F7:讀檔選單。[[F8:切換中文(16/24)/英文原文。[[F9:重新開始遊戲。[[F12:儲存螢幕擷圖。[[CTRL-Q:離開遊戲。

column1: Like all of Spielburg's citizens, we were most impressed when you dispelled the enchantment upon Elsa von Spielburg and broke the curse on the Baron by defeating Baba Yaga.
column2: 如同史畢柏格所有的子民,你解除艾莎·馮·史畢柏格身上的魔咒、又擊敗芭芭雅嘎破除男爵的詛咒時,我們都深深折服。

column1: It seems to have no effect.
column2: 似乎毫無作用。

column1: I craft experimental necklaces from a material I call fool's iron. When wet, the gold resembles ordinary iron to the untrained eye. I believe it may prove useful to prevent them from being stolen during a burglary.
column2: 我用一種我管它叫「愚人鐵」的材料,打造實驗性的項鍊。沾了水,那金子在外行人眼裡就跟普通鐵沒兩樣。我相信這能防它們在竊案中被偷走。

column1: It is not a paladin's nature to judge others, but I would strongly advise you to be wary of who you announce your profession to. Many people in this city are leery of thieves after the tragedy that befell Raseir.
column2: 妄加評斷他人並非聖騎士的本性,但我要鄭重勸你:對誰表明你的行當,可得當心。拉希爾那場慘劇之後,這城裡許多人對盜賊都心存戒備。

column1: Like all of Spielburg's citizens, we were most impressed when you dispelled the enchantment upon Elsa von Spielburg and caused the defeat of the Brigands.
column2: 如同史畢柏格所有的子民,你解除艾莎·馮·史畢柏格身上的魔咒、又促成強盜團覆滅時,我們都深深折服。

column1: Many forget the polite thing to do,[  Greetings, too often, are given by few.[I accept and confide, in your gesture with pride,[  The same I extend now to you.
column2: 多少人忘了該有的禮數,[  問候之語,難得有人付出。[我欣然領受,並引以為傲地信賴你的舉止,[  此刻,我也以同樣的問候回贈於你。

column1: Maybe you could use some magical fire to burn magical earth? I do have some powder of burning that might work. But it is very rare, and it makes such pretty flames! I'd hate to have it wasted.
column2: 也許你可以用些魔法之火去燒魔法之土?我倒有些燃燒之粉,或許管用。但它非常稀有,綻放的火焰又那麼美!我可捨不得它被浪費。

column1: Mirror your opponent's fighting style. Mount an offense to break through his guard and retaliate with counterattacks if he becomes more aggressive.
column2: 把對手的打法照樣奉還。發動攻勢突破他的防禦;他若轉趨兇猛,就以反擊回敬。

column1: My second cousin Sashanan the jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace, and my third cousin once removed, Tiram the rug merchant, were unable to set up their stands today. An Elemental of Air is filling the plaza!
column2: 我那在王宮廣場賣珠寶的二表親薩沙南,還有我那隔了輩的三表親、賣地毯的提拉姆,今天都沒法擺攤。一頭風元素精靈正盤踞著整座廣場!

column1: My spine shivers at the thought of so much battle with the fiendish Ghouls, but despite your bravery, I regret that my larder of Ghoul claws is full, and I can purchase no more.
column2: 一想到要跟那些兇殘的食屍鬼這般廝殺,我就脊背發涼。可儘管你英勇過人,很遺憾,我儲爪的庫房已經滿了,沒法再收。

column1: My thanks that you returned my purse,[Although it seems that it is much the worse[For a thief has stolen what was there[And gives me back a purse that now is bare.
column2: 謝你把我的錢袋送回,[只是它的下場似乎更悲——[小偷偷走了裡頭的一切,[還我一只如今空空的錢袋。

column1: My wallets are perfect for carrying large amounts of money without weighing you down. Buy one and it is guaranteed you shall feel much lighter than before.
column2: 我的錢包用來裝大筆鈔票卻不壓身,再合適不過。買一個,保你覺得比先前輕快許多。

column1: Nevertheless, I'm holding a sale in which I'm set on selling my sole remaining Saurus to some susceptible schmuck. This is truly your lucky day!
column2: 話雖如此,我正辦著一場特賣,鐵了心要把我僅剩的一頭索魯斯賣給某個好騙的冤大頭。今天可真是你的幸運日!

column1: Nevertheless, you will need to find her before you can buy anything here. You may have less trouble finding her if the names of the streets make sense to you.
column2: 話雖如此,你得先找到她,才能在這兒買東西。你若弄懂了街名的意思,找她或許就沒那麼費勁。

column1: Once your opponent starts advancing, approach him in order to avoid terrain loss. A safe way to battle is to stay inside melee range until your opponent attempts an attack.
column2: 對手一開始進逼,你就上前迎他,以免失了地利。一種穩妥的打法,是守在近身範圍內,直到對手出招。

column1: Only the Katta can stand to work the bazaars in the summer. The rest of the year, you cannot walk through the plazas, there are so many merchants.
column2: 也只有卡塔族受得了夏天在市集做生意。一年裡其餘時節,廣場上商人多得讓你寸步難行。

column1: Our fresh dates are stuffed with the finest of almonds, carefully roasted over the brazier, and then glazed in honey. We personally stuff the dates ourselves.
column2: 我們的鮮椰棗塞著最上等的杏仁,在炭爐上細細烤過,再裹上蜂蜜。每一顆都是我們親手填的。

column1: Our kin in Shapeir had not forgotten about us. They sent a great deal of trade items along with the very next caravan heading for Spielburg. That happened to be Abdulla's caravan.
column2: 我們在夏皮爾的族人並沒忘了我們。他們託下一支前往史畢柏格的商隊捎來大批貨物。那正好是阿布杜拉的商隊。

column1: Our people can fight, if necessary, but because of our small stature we see battle as a last resort, rather than a way to gain individual glory.
column2: 必要時我們族人也能戰,但因身形矮小,我們視戰鬥為最後手段,而非博取個人榮耀的途徑。

column1: Over the past year, despite the excitement of seeing new lands and cultures, I have felt the pangs of homesickness quite often. But I have never felt regret being by Shameen's side.
column2: 過去這一年,儘管見識新天地、新風土令人興奮,我仍時常嘗到鄉愁的滋味。但守在沙明身邊,我從未後悔過。

column1: Perhaps it was the prospect of traveling through the freezing mountain area again that kept us in Spielburg. Or perhaps it was a glimmer of hope that we would still find what we were seeking.
column2: 或許是想到要再次穿越那冰封的山區,才把我們留在了史畢柏格。又或許,是那一線「終能尋得所求」的微光。

column1: Pity. Ah, well, there will be others. Good night, sir.
column2: 可惜。唉,算了,還會有別人的。晚安,先生。

column1: Rakeesh also has powerful strike he call Ankh Slash. Hits three times. Hurts plenty. Rakeesh need to concentrate to use it. Watch him carefully. Rakeesh lose concentration when you dodge or parry initial swing.
column2: 拉基什還有一記威猛的攻擊,他管它叫「安卡斬」。連擊三下,痛得很。拉基什得凝神才使得出來。仔細盯著他。你閃開或招架住他頭一揮,他就會分神。

column1: Rakeesh be paladin and only fights to defend himself. He always give opponent room to retreat. Never corners people. If you be tired, retreating to corner will grant you quick break.
column2: 拉基什是聖騎士,只為自衛而戰。他總給對手留下後退的餘地,從不把人逼到角落。你要是累了,退到角落能讓你喘口氣。

column1: Regardless, we decided to stay in Spielburg for the winter and used our remaining funds to set up what you came to know as the Hero's Tale Inn.
column2: 無論如何,我們決定在史畢柏格過冬,用剩下的錢開了你後來認識的那家「英雄傳說客棧」。

column1: Seeing that every self-respecting monster would be able to out-bite, out-scratch and out-claw you, keeping your only means of evening the odds in your possession would be smart.
column2: 鑒於任何有點自尊的怪物,在啃咬、抓撓、揮爪上都贏得了你,留著你唯一能扳平劣勢的本錢,才是聰明之舉。

column1: Shema and I heard several stories about Erana as we made our way from Shapeir to Spielburg. Particularly in the areas near Sangerhafen and Mordavia, tales about her were plentiful.
column2: 雪瑪和我從夏皮爾趕往史畢柏格的途中,聽過好幾則伊瑞娜的故事。尤其在桑格哈芬與莫達維亞一帶,關於她的傳說多不勝數。

column1: In this place, you must rely upon only your magic.
column2: 在這個地方,你只能仰賴你的魔法。

column1: Powder of burning is a really excellent mixture of potassium nitrate from bat guano, powdered charcoal, sulfur, and ambergris. The ambergris doesn't really do anything, but it makes the powder smell more interesting.
column2: 燃燒之粉,是一種上好的混合物,由蝙蝠糞提煉的硝酸鉀、炭粉、硫磺和龍涎香調成。龍涎香其實沒什麼作用,只是讓這粉聞起來更有意思些。

column1: So it seems that things now have come full circle. The journey Shema and I made for the future of our homeland has ended and yours is about to begin.
column2: 看來如今一切已繞了一整圈。雪瑪和我為家鄉未來而踏上的旅程已然結束,而你的旅程正要開始。

column1: Something in the back of your mind tells you to deal with the problems in Raseir before dispelling the Emir.
column2: 你腦海深處有個聲音告訴你,在解除埃米爾的魔咒之前,先處理拉希爾的麻煩。

column1: So you made the gust bite the dust, Russ? Look, it's neat that you stalled a repeat by causing its defeat, Pete. But keep the bellows, Fellow, and now that the Elemental's mellow you can put it to good use, Bruce.
column2: 這麼說,你讓那陣狂風一敗塗地啦,小羅?瞧,你把它打敗、攔下重演,真有一手,小彼。不過風箱你留著吧,老兄;如今那元素精靈乖了,你正好好好派它用場,小布。

column1: Speaking of health, a Saurus can save your life. Let's say you're traveling in the desert, you're lost, and you've run out of food and water. You are crawling pathetically and hopelessly in the blazing desert sun.
column2: 說到保命,一頭索魯斯能救你一命。比方說,你在沙漠裡趕路,迷了路,食物和水都見了底。你正在炙熱的沙漠驕陽下,可憐又絕望地爬行著。

column1: Special today only, I sell you a genuine made-in-Japan, glow-in-dark turban which says, 'My father went to Shapeir and all he brought me was...'
column2: 今天限定特賣,我賣你一條正宗日本製的夜光頭巾,上頭寫著:「我爸去了夏皮爾,結果只帶回給我這個……」

column1: Sticking your shoomflufluf out sideways, you touch your bozombie (wet side up, of course) to your flanigoogle, being careful not to cut off the circulation to your capitalary artery.
column2: 你把你的「咻夫拉夫」往旁邊一伸,讓你的「波喪比」(當然啦,濕的那面朝上)碰上你的「法尼咕咕」,還得小心別切斷通往「資本動脈」的血流。

column1: Talk about over-exerting yourself... that last little maneuver wore you out so much it hurt. In fact, it hurt so much, it killed you. Take it easier next time.
column2: 這就叫用力過猛了……剛才那一小招把你累得發疼。事實上,疼到把你給疼死了。下回悠著點。

column1: Thank you, a thousand thank-yous. It is with your support that I may continue my vital work.
column2: 謝謝你,千恩萬謝。全靠你的支持,我才能繼續這要緊的工作。

column1: That is one thing, however, that it does very well. It cannot truly feel pain and thus will keep attacking, even while you are damaging it at the same time. Fight with caution.
column2: 不過,有一件事它倒做得很到位:它感覺不到真正的痛,所以即便你同時在傷它,它仍會不停攻擊。作戰時務必謹慎。

column1: That's one way of getting more iron in your diet.
column2: 這倒是替三餐多補點鐵質的法子。

column1: That's one way of getting more junk food in your diet.
column2: 這倒是替三餐多添點垃圾食物的法子。

column1: That sounds most interesting. It is probably not a natural creature, but around here, who can tell? You could try a dispel potion on it, though.
column2: 聽起來真有意思。它八成不是自然生物,不過這一帶,誰說得準呢?你倒可以拿驅散藥水試試它。

column1: The bazaar is wherever the merchants are. In the summer only the Katta can stand the heat. Other times of year, you cannot move in the plazas for so many stands and shops.
column2: 商人在哪兒,市集就在哪兒。夏天裡,也只有卡塔族受得了那熱。一年其餘時節,廣場上攤子店鋪多得讓你動彈不得。

column1: The Caravan Master informed us that recent snowfall had already made further passage through the Dragonsmoke Mountains impossible and that the caravan would head back towards Wien.
column2: 商隊隊長通知我們,近日的降雪已使穿越龍煙山的去路斷絕,商隊將折返維恩。

column1: The city walls are too slick to scale and you have no business on the rooftops anyway.
column2: 城牆太滑,爬不上去,再說屋頂上也沒你的事。

column1: The coffee has been boiled for hours, it would appear, and it best resembles brown ooze. You can understand why people remember Raseirish Coffee.
column2: 這咖啡看樣子已經煮了好幾個鐘頭,最像一灘褐色的黏液。你這下明白,人們為何對「拉希爾咖啡」念念不忘了。

column1: Every time we reached a new land, we sent a letter back to our kin in Shapeir to let them know our whereabouts. And every time we stopped in a new land, we visited the Adventurer's Guild in order to learn about the local news.
column2: 每抵達一處新地方,我們就捎封信回夏皮爾給族人,好讓他們知道我們的下落。每在一處新地方落腳,我們也都會去當地的冒險者公會,打聽本地的消息。

column1: Magic, magic everywhere. Find it at Aziza's lair. Magic, magic, wait a bit. Find it in the soul of WIT. Magic, magic in the stars. The Astrologer can see that far. Magic, magic, I dare say, it's even in the games we play.
column2: 魔法魔法,無處不在。阿姿莎的居所裡尋它。魔法魔法,且慢些。巫師技術學院的魂魄裡尋它。魔法魔法,藏於星辰。占星師看得那般遠。魔法魔法,我敢說,連我們玩的遊戲裡也有它。

column1: The Elemental's not going to fit in that little basket in its current form, but you certainly will if you insist on taking this approach.
column2: 那元素精靈以目前的形態塞不進那小籃子——但你要是硬要這麼幹,你倒是塞得進去。

column1: The Enable/Disable button: This button turns the copilot on and off. On the hardest arcade difficulty level, the copilot can not be enabled.[[The Close button: Closes the copilot menu.[[The Help button: Displays this message.
column2: 啟用/停用鈕:此鈕開關戰鬥輔助。在最高的街機難度下,無法啟用輔助。[[關閉鈕:關閉輔助選單。[[說明鈕:顯示本訊息。

column1: THE ICON BAR[[When your cursor is moved to the top of the screen the icon bar appears. The visible icons are WALK, LOOK, USE, TALK, SPECIAL ACTIONS, MAGIC, LAST-USED ITEM, INVENTORY, OPTIONS, and HELP.
column2: 圖示列[[當你把游標移到畫面頂端,圖示列便會出現。看得見的圖示有:行走、查看、使用、交談、特殊動作、魔法、上次使用的物品、物品欄、選項,以及說明。

column1: The item Saba sold you is a disk that's made of many small strings of reed carefully woven together. The image on the weaving shows a familiar face.
column2: 薩巴賣給你的是個圓盤,用許多細小的蘆葦條精心編成。編織上的圖案是一張熟悉的臉。

column1: The man glares back at you. This is not the face you associate with bartenders. It reminds you more of Bruno from Spielburg: definitely not a man to mess with.
column2: 那人回瞪你一眼。這可不是你印象中酒保該有的臉,倒更讓你想起史畢柏格的布魯諾——絕對是個惹不得的傢伙。

column1: The map only works in the streets and plazas of Shapeir.
column2: 地圖只在夏皮爾的街道與廣場上管用。

column1: The message reads:[["Those who wish to follow the way of the sword should start at the very beginning or face their undoing."
column2: 上頭寫著:[[「欲循劍道者,當自最初步起,否則必招敗亡。」

column1: The mountain pass opened up a few weeks before you arrived in Spielburg. We had our very first guest stay over around that time. Unfortunately, as you know, he was no longer in a position to pay for his room and board.
column2: 山隘在你抵達史畢柏格前幾週才開通。差不多那時候,我們迎來了第一位過夜的客人。可惜,你也知道,他後來已無力支付食宿費了。

column1: The beast is much too large to fetch.
column2: 這野獸太大了,「取物」術搬不動。

column1: Even though you are not versed in swordsmanship, you have excellent skills with the dagger. Mix in offensive and defensive tactics and throw in the occasional long-range attack. Keep the enemy guessing what your next action will be.
column2: 你雖不諳劍術,匕首卻使得極好。攻守兼施,偶爾再來記遠距攻擊,讓敵人摸不透你下一步要幹什麼。

column1: I am trying to develop a pill which will protect against the effect of the claws of the Ghoul. I have not been able to get many claws, for few Ghouls are willing to give them to me, and few adventurers are willing to get them for me.
column2: 我正試著研發一種抵禦食屍鬼爪子效力的藥丸。可惜爪子我弄不到多少——願意給的食屍鬼沒幾隻,願意替我去取的冒險者也沒幾個。

column1: Katta do not necessarily dismiss prophecies. We believe that one's fate lies within oneself and is not predestined, regardless of what omens say. If anything, they indicate a possible outcome, but not one that will come to pass on its own.
column2: 卡塔族並不一概否定預言。我們相信,一個人的命運繫於自身,而非命中註定,無論徵兆怎麼說。徵兆頂多指出一種可能的結局,卻不會自行應驗。

column1: Magical games are an entertaining way to keep one's magical skills and mind in shape. My personal game of choice is my own interpretation of the Wizard's Whirl game. If you are interested in a friendly magical match, please let me know.
column2: 魔法遊戲是讓人保持法術技藝與頭腦靈光的有趣方式。我個人偏好的是自己改編版的「巫師之旋」。你若有意來場友好的魔法較量,儘管告訴我。

column1: MAGIC MENU[[The Magic Menu lists all the spells that your hero has learnt. Simply click on a spell to cast it. Available spells are ZAP, CALM, DAZZLE, DETECT MAGIC, FETCH, FLAME DART, FORCE BOLT, LEVITATE, OPEN, REVERSAL, and TRIGGER.
column2: 魔法選單[[魔法選單列出你的英雄已學會的所有法術。點一下法術即可施放。可用的法術有:電擊、鎮定、炫目、偵測魔法、取物、火鏢、力箭、飄浮、開啟、反轉,以及觸發。

column1: Perhaps after hearing our tale, you now understand that you did more than just become our traveling companion. You allowed us to return home with pride and gave a sense of purpose to the hardships we had endured over the past year.
column2: 聽完我們的故事,或許你如今明白,你不僅僅是成了我們的旅伴。你讓我們得以光榮返鄉,也賦予了我們過去這一年所受的苦難一份意義。

column1: That is most kind of you, but I do not think I would have a use for it. The tricky thing with salves, pills, and potions is that each region requires its own recipe, for none of the components used to make that oil are available here.
column2: 你真是太好心了,但我想我用不上它。藥膏、藥丸和藥水麻煩就麻煩在:每個地方都得用自己的方子,因為製那種油的原料,這兒一樣也沒有。

column1: THE ICONS[[TALK: Position the talk cursor on the person (or thing) you want to speak to. If conversation is possible, it will begin.[[LAST-USED ITEM: Displays the last inventory item you selected. Click it when you want to use this item.
column2: 圖示說明[[交談:把交談游標移到你想說話的人(或物)身上。若能交談,對話便會開始。[[上次使用的物品:顯示你上回選取的物品。想用這件物品時點它即可。

column1: Then, wait, You see your faithful Saurus running up to you. You are saved! You can eat Saurus steaks for a week. In fact, you can set up a concession stand right there and make a tidy sum off of Saurus steaks.
column2: 然後,且慢——你看見你忠實的索魯斯朝你奔來。你得救了!你可以吃上一週的索魯斯排。其實啊,你大可就地擺個小攤,靠賣索魯斯排賺上一筆。

column1: Shema will be with you shortly.
column2: 雪瑪很快就會來招呼你。

column1: I do not think there was any part of our journey where I felt more miserable than during the week it took us to reach Spielburg. The icy winds blowing through the pass chilled me to the bone and I had a lot of trouble breathing the thin mountain air.
column2: 我們整段旅程裡,我想沒有哪一段比抵達史畢柏格那一週更難熬了。隘口颳來的冰風冷得我透骨,稀薄的山間空氣也讓我喘不過氣。

column1: Join our 12-day horticulture seminar and learn all you'll ever need to know about making your hedge garden grow and getting results first time, every time! With "Personal Flower: Pet the Hedge," we do all the hard work so that you won't have to!
column2: 報名我們十二天的園藝研習營,學會讓你的綠籬花園生長所需的一切,次次見效、回回成功!有了「私房花卉:寵愛你的綠籬」,粗活我們全包,你什麼都不必做!

column1: KEYS AND INSTRUCTIONS.[[CTRL-S: Show your Hero's statistics sheet.[[CTRL-R: Displays a map of the alley system.[[BackSpace or CTRL-H: Toggles your compass on and off.[[CTRL-T: Show the curent time and day.[[CTRL-I or TAB: Show inventory list (SCI style).
column2: 按鍵與說明。[[CTRL-S:顯示你英雄的屬性表。[[CTRL-R:顯示巷弄系統的地圖。[[退格鍵或 CTRL-H:開關你的羅盤。[[CTRL-T:顯示當前時辰與日期。[[CTRL-I 或 TAB:顯示物品清單(SCI 樣式)。

column1: Learning about these things was most unsettling, for we did not know if the letters we had sent our kin had arrived in Shapeir safely or not. And without traders and tourists, we would never make enough money from our inn to leave Spielburg.
column2: 得知這些事讓我們心緒難安,因為我們不知道捎給族人的信究竟有沒有平安送抵夏皮爾。而沒有商人與遊客,我們靠客棧永遠也賺不夠離開史畢柏格的錢。

column1: Seeing that you do not excel in fighting technique, your best strategy may be to avoid hanging out in melee range too much. On the other hand, getting cornered is a predicament you should avoid at all costs, as it will be very hard to get out of.
column2: 鑒於你的戰鬥技巧並不出眾,最佳策略或許是別在近身範圍逗留太久。另一方面,被逼到角落是你務必不惜代價避免的困境,因為那極難脫身。

column1: THE ICON BAR[[The icons are all standard Sierra Icon Interface, and work the same way as Sierra's old point-and-click adventure games. Left-click to perform the action of the currently selected cursor. Right-click to cycle through available cursors.
column2: 圖示列[[這些圖示全是標準的 Sierra 圖示介面,操作方式與 Sierra 早期的點選式冒險遊戲相同。左鍵執行當前選取游標的動作。右鍵則在可用的游標間循環切換。

column1: THE ICONS[[HELP: Displays this help screen.[[OPTIONS: When pressed, several options will be displayed: SAVE, RESTORE, RESTART, QUIT, AGS, ABOUT (?), PLAY, GAME SKILL, SOUND VOLUME, MUSIC VOLUME, GAME SPEED, COMBAT OPTIONS, DIALOGUE SYSTEM, and SILLY CLOWNS.
column2: 圖示說明[[說明:顯示此說明畫面。[[選項:按下後會顯示數個選項:存檔、讀檔、重新開始、離開、AGS、關於(?)、繼續、遊戲難度、音效音量、音樂音量、遊戲速度、戰鬥選項、對話系統,以及「傻氣小丑」。

column1: THE ICONS[[WALK: Click this icon to move the Hero where you want him to walk.[[LOOK: Click this on specific things to receive a description about them.[[USE: Use this to pick items up and also to interact with your environment (e.g. opening a door).
column2: 圖示說明[[行走:點此圖示,讓英雄走到你要他去的地方。[[查看:對特定事物點這個,以取得它們的描述。[[使用:用它來撿拾物品,也用來與環境互動(例如開門)。

column1: The Offense Slider: The further this slider is set to the right, the further the copilot will tend to concentrate on attack-related assistance.
column2: 攻擊滑桿:此滑桿越往右設,戰鬥輔助就越傾向專注於與攻擊相關的協助。

column1: The only ingredient I have run short of are dark Terrorsaurus scales. The scales of a male Terrorsaurus become much darker during mating season and make potent pill components. Unfortunately, Saurus mating season is still several months away.
column2: 我唯一短缺的材料是深色的恐魯斯鱗片。公恐魯斯的鱗片在交配季會變得深得多,是效力強勁的藥丸成分。可惜,索魯斯的交配季還要好幾個月才到。

column1: Greetings, friend of the Katta.
column2: 你好,卡塔族的朋友。

column1: A discarded barrel rests nearby.
column2: 附近擱著一只廢棄的木桶。

column1: A strong sense of magic radiates from the Elemental.
column2: 那元素精靈散發著強烈的魔法氣息。

column1: As you expected, the door is locked.
column2: 不出你所料,門鎖著。

column1: I accept your challenge gladly, my friend.
column2: 我樂意接受你的挑戰,朋友。

column1: I have already given you my very last speck of it.
column2: 我最後一點點都已經給你了。

column1: Inside, you have stored the essence of the Pizza Elemental.
column2: 裡頭裝著你封存的「披薩元素精靈」精華。

column1: It takes real muscle to be good at arm wrestling.
column2: 比腕力要比得好,得有真本事的肌肉。

column1: Keep off the grass.
column2: 「請勿踐踏草坪。」

column1: Obey the laws at all times.
column2: 「時刻遵守律法。」

column1: That is not an appropriate sponsor.
column2: 那不是個合適的引薦人。

column1: That is not a valid alley room number.
column2: 那不是個有效的巷弄房號。

column1: That wouldn't be a good idea.
column2: 這主意不妙。

column1: The Brigand is no longer in any condition to reply.
column2: 那強盜已經沒法回話了。

column1: The door is locked.
column2: 門鎖著。

column1: The map only works in the streets and plazas.
column2: 地圖只在街道與廣場上管用。

column1: There is a strong sense of magic upon the Fire Elemental.
column2: 火元素精靈身上有股強烈的魔法氣息。

column1: There is only silence from beyond the door.
column2: 門後唯有一片寂靜。

column1: There is only silence from beyond the door. The occupants must be sleeping.
column2: 門後唯有一片寂靜。屋裡的人想必睡了。

column1: There's no time for that.
column2: 沒空管那個。

column1: There's no time for that now!
column2: 現在沒空管那個!

column1: There's no time for that now.
column2: 現在沒空管那個。

column1: These traditional windows are square-shaped and have been built into the wall.
column2: 這些傳統樣式的窗子呈方形,嵌在牆裡。

column1: The soles of your boots show signs of heavy deterioration.
column2: 你的靴底已顯出嚴重磨損的跡象。

column1: May you have a good day today.
column2: 願你今天順心如意。

column1: My name is Lira.
column2: 我叫莉拉。

column1: I am trying to develop a pill which will protect against the effect of the claws of the Ghoul.
column2: 我正試著研發一種藥丸,用來抵禦食屍鬼爪子的效力。

column1: I don't have the bellows anymore. Someone must have figured they needed them badly enough to steal them.
column2: 我沒風箱了。準是有人覺得非要不可,把它偷走了。

column1: I still need the "Fruit of Compassion." You need to add the victim's hair at the last moment.
column2: 我還需要「慈悲之果」。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻加進去。

column1: The guards look even more stone-faced than usual. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 那些衛兵的臉比平常更鐵青。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: The item Saba sold you is a disk that's made of many small strings of reed carefully woven together.
column2: 薩巴賣給你的是個圓盤,用許多細小的蘆葦條精心編成。

column1: As with all of our kin, Shema and I are desert dwellers and unaccustomed to colder climates. We were having increasing difficulty dealing with the declining temperature as we traveled northwest through Sangerhafen.
column2: 和我們所有族人一樣,雪瑪和我都是沙漠之民,不慣寒冷的氣候。我們往西北穿越桑格哈芬時,越來越難招架那一路下滑的氣溫。

column1: Before our caravan left Spielburg, we wrote a letter to our kin, telling them we were alright and requesting support to continue our journey after the winter. We paid the Caravan Master to have it delivered to Shapeir.
column2: 我們的商隊離開史畢柏格前,我們寫了封信給族人,報平安,並請他們支援我們在冬後繼續旅程。我們付錢託商隊隊長把信送到夏皮爾。

column1: Despite our own sorrow, Shameen and I felt deeply for Abdulla. At the very least we still had a place to spend the night and a modest means of making income. We offered him our hospitality free of charge for as long as necessary.
column2: 儘管自身也有愁苦,沙明和我仍深深同情阿布杜拉。至少我們還有過夜的地方,還有一份微薄的營生。我們免費招待他,要住多久都行。

column1: Indeed. That remote region was ideal terrain for Brigands planning ambushes. During the colder months, avalanches are common and the paths are blocked by snow. In warmer months, monsters that reside in the forest during winter return to their habitual territory in the mountains.
column2: 正是。那偏遠之地是強盜設伏的絕佳地形。較冷的月份雪崩頻仍,道路為雪所阻;較暖的月份,冬天棲身森林的怪物則重返牠們在山中慣常的領地。

column1: Its eyes catch yours, and you can't seem to look away. Huge eyes, burning eyes that see your very soul. You seem to hear in your mind the words, "Come closer, much closer. Come closer and release me, free me. Come closer and trust me. Look into my eyes and trust me."
column2: 牠的雙眼攫住了你的目光,你彷彿移不開視線。那雙巨大、灼灼燃燒的眼,直視你的靈魂深處。你似乎在腦中聽見一個聲音:「靠近些,再靠近些。靠近些,放了我,還我自由。靠近些,信我。看著我的眼睛,信我。」

column1: The creature before you looks like a cross between a centaur and a lion. His muscular upper torso and leonine features add a certain ferocity to his appearance, but his eyes look kindly and calmly back at you. You notice a bandage is wrapped around his hind leg.
column2: 你面前這頭生物,看上去像半人馬與獅子的混血。他結實的上半身與獅形容貌,為他平添幾分兇猛,那雙眼睛卻和善而平靜地回望你。你注意到他的一條後腿纏著繃帶。

column1: The only monsters we saw there were of the stuffed variety on the walls of the Guild Hall. We did not dare to venture into the forest of Spielburg.
column2: 我們在那兒見到的怪物,就只有公會大廳牆上那些做成標本的。我們可不敢踏進史畢柏格的森林。

column1: The only other items inside the game box are some floppy disks and a glossy catalogue advertising upcoming Sierra On-Line games. None of these are important to your quest, however, so you close the box again.
column2: 遊戲盒裡其餘的東西,不過是幾片磁片,和一本宣傳 Sierra On-Line 即將推出遊戲的光面型錄。這些對你的任務都無關緊要,於是你又把盒子闔上。

column1: THE OPTIONS MENU[[SAVE: Saves the game.[[RESTORE: Restores a previously saved game.[[RESTART: Restarts the game again from the beginning.[[QUIT: Stop Playing.[[AGS: Information about the AGS Engine.[[ABOUT: Information about this game.[[PLAY: Return to the game.
column2: 選項選單[[存檔:儲存遊戲。[[讀檔:讀取先前儲存的遊戲。[[重新開始:從頭再玩一次。[[離開:停止遊戲。[[AGS:關於 AGS 引擎的資訊。[[關於:關於本遊戲的資訊。[[繼續:返回遊戲。

column1: The 'painting' Lisha made has been constructed with dried flowers and shows a blooming Julanar in her desert alcove. You wonder how Lisha found out about that.
column2: 莉莎做的這幅「畫」是用乾燥花拼成的,描繪著茱拉娜爾在沙漠壁龕中綻放的模樣。你納悶莉莎是怎麼知道這事的。

column1: There are tales told to me of a woman transformed into a plant through a Djinni's spell. She stands like a tree somewhere out in the desert, but it is said she bears no fruit.
column2: 有人對我說起一個女子被鎮尼的法術變成植物的故事。她像棵樹般佇立在沙漠某處,但據說她不結果。

column1: There is no higher-quality moisturizer or body oil in the world. The content of every bottle you see here was hand-made by myself, and nothing I sell contains any chemical components whatsoever.
column2: 世上再沒有品質更高的潤膚乳或身體油了。你眼前每一瓶的內容物都是我親手製作,我賣的東西絕不含任何化學成分。

column1: There is some form of magical protection around the cage which prevents me from detecting it. It is clear that a powerful magic user intends the creature to die. The beast is probably enchanted. You will need a dispel potion to find its true form.
column2: 牢籠四周有某種魔法防護,害我偵測不到它。顯然有個強大的法師蓄意要這生物死。那野獸八成中了魔法。你需要一瓶驅散藥水,才能找出牠的真身。

column1: There's no point cramming your storage chest full with pebbles. You'd be better off dropping them and getting more from the desert if you need them.
column2: 把儲物箱塞滿小石子毫無意義。你不如把它們丟了,需要時再從沙漠裡撿。

column1: These cases probably contained hedges long ago. The blackened ash inside of the containers suggests that those hedges were burned down at some point in the past.
column2: 這些花盆從前大概種過綠籬。盆裡焦黑的灰燼顯示,那些綠籬曾在某個時候被燒毀。

column1: These traditional windows are square-shaped and have been built into the wall. Heavy iron bars cover them.
column2: 這些傳統樣式的方形窗嵌在牆裡,外頭還罩著厚重的鐵條。

column1: The sheriff was a very kind man who had dinner at our inn at times together with his wife and assistant. He was always very eager to listen to tales about our homeland.
column2: 那位郡長是個很和善的人,偶爾會帶著妻子和助手來我們客棧用晚餐。他總是熱切地想聽我們家鄉的故事。

column1: The Sidestep/Misc Slider: When this slider favors Sidestep, the copilot will favor sidesteps to avoid attacks, keeping the hero in melee range. When this slider favors Misc, the copilot will try to duck or jump an attack if possible and will otherwise jump out of melee range.
column2: 側移/其他滑桿:此桿偏向「側移」時,輔助會傾向以側步閃避攻擊,讓英雄保持在近身範圍。偏向「其他」時,輔助會盡量蹲下或跳過攻擊,否則便跳出近身範圍。

column1: The sign hints at the presence of a formerly successful business. Unfortunately, it appears that the regime change took its toll on this as well.
column2: 這招牌暗示著一樁昔日興旺的買賣。可惜,政權更迭似乎也讓它付出了代價。

column1: The silver tea service is very finely wrought and was once the property of Kareem bin Ma'amar. It's a good thing you didn't take Kareem with your tea.
column2: 這套銀茶具做工極其精細,曾是卡里姆·賓·馬阿瑪爾的財產。幸好你沒把卡里姆連茶一起「取」了去。

column1: The stall has been empty for months at least. If the merchant left in a hurry, the stall must have been looted of its contents long before your arrival.
column2: 這攤子至少空了好幾個月。那商人若是匆忙離去,攤上的貨想必早在你到來之前就被洗劫一空了。

column1: The strange man decided to leave town with his friend of the golden hair and strange social behavior. He said something about going to 'Fredonia.'
column2: 那怪人決定和他那位金髮、舉止古怪的朋友一同離城。他提了一句要去「弗雷多尼亞」。

column1: The Sultan has always been deeply concerned about the situation, but has not sent any more men for fear of losing them also. All he has been able to do is shelter those who fled or were driven from the city.
column2: 蘇丹一直深為局勢憂心,卻因怕再折損人馬而不敢多派兵。他能做的,只有收容那些逃離或被逐出城的人。

column1: The supper tonight is Saurus stuffed with grapes and saffron rice, and honeyed rabbit. This is a specialty of Shapeir and I hope you will like it.
column2: 今晚的晚餐是釀了葡萄與番紅花飯的索魯斯,還有蜜汁兔肉。這是夏皮爾的招牌菜,希望你會喜歡。

column1: The window's marble casement shows cracks in many places and the curtain resembles a faded rag. It's obvious this residence has seen better days.
column2: 這扇窗的大理石窗框多處龜裂,窗簾像塊褪色的破布。顯然,這宅子早已風光不再。

column1: The woman fits in well with the rest of the Guild Hall, but would stand out like a sore thumb in your average Shapeirian crowd. Her appearance gives you the impression that she's as foreign to these parts as you are.
column2: 這女子待在公會大廳裡毫不突兀,擱進一般的夏皮爾人群中卻會格外扎眼。她的相貌給你一種印象:她跟你一樣,是這一帶的外鄉人。

column1: The woodwork in this door has seen better days, but on the whole, the condition of the door seems better than the overall condition of the city.
column2: 這門的木工早已風光不再,但整體看來,這門的狀況似乎還勝過這座城市的整體景況。

column1: The xX Slash button: When assisting the player with follow-up slashes after a swing, this button will determine the maximum amount of slashes the copilot will attempt. This button will not work if the player does not have the ability to string attacks together.
column2: 連斬鈕:在協助玩家於揮砍後追加連斬時,此鈕決定輔助嘗試的最大連斬次數。若玩家不具備串連攻擊的能力,此鈕將不起作用。

column1: They are intelligent and brave creatures, willing to obey the slightest command from their master. They will take you wherever you wish and come to your aid against monsters.
column2: 牠們是聰慧而勇敢的生物,願服從主人最細微的指令。牠們會載你去任何你想去的地方,並在你對抗怪物時前來相助。

column1: Thieves have a variety of special skills, such as picking locks, hiding, and climbing walls. The thief tends to work around problems, rather than attacking them directly.
column2: 盜賊身懷各種特殊技能,例如開鎖、藏匿與攀牆。盜賊傾向繞過問題,而非正面硬碰。

column1: Thinking of all the injuries and wounds you WOULD have sustained without this armor protecting you is enough to cause second thoughts about dropping your leather jerkin.
column2: 一想到若沒這身盔甲護著,你「本該」受的那些傷,就足以讓你對丟掉皮背心一事重新考慮。

column1: This door must have been in poor state even before the city fell into decline. In its current state, its only real use would be to provide a meal for the local termite population.
column2: 這門想必在這城市沒落之前就已殘破不堪。就它如今的狀態,唯一的實際用途,大概就是供本地的白蟻飽餐一頓。

column1: This door must have provided entry to a wealthy home in the past. The bars blocking the door give you the impression the residence has been abandoned for quite some time.
column2: 這門從前想必是通往一戶富貴人家的入口。封門的鐵條給你一種印象:這宅子已荒廢了好一陣子。

column1: This genuine imitation brass secret membership badge, with its secret code and secret runic engraving (so secret that NOBODY knows what it means), marks you as an EOF member in good standing.
column2: 這枚正宗仿黃銅的祕密會員徽章,帶著祕密暗號與祕密符文刻紋(祕密到沒有「任何人」知道它是什麼意思),標誌著你是永恆戰士團(EOF)聲譽良好的一員。

column1: This is not a good place to cast your Fetch spell.
column2: 這裡不是施「取物」術的好地方。

column1: This is not a good place to cast your Trigger spell.
column2: 這裡不是施「觸發」術的好地方。

column1: I have a substance called powder of burning that might be of some use against an Earth Elemental, but I should think that someone with your particular skills would be able to think of another way to defeat the Earth Elemental. I consider it much too valuable to be wasted needlessly.
column2: 我有種叫燃燒之粉的東西,對付土元素精靈或許有點用,但我想,憑你那身本事,該想得出別的法子來擊敗它。我認為它太珍貴,不該白白浪費。

column1: This is not a good place to practice that spell.
column2: 這裡不是練那個法術的好地方。

column1: This is not a good place to practice your magic.
column2: 這裡不是練魔法的好地方。

column1: This is not a good place to rest.
column2: 這裡不是個歇腳的好地方。

column1: This is ridiculous. Here I am, a man of unquestionable integrity and dignity, forced to sell his sole remaining Saurus for a mere fraction of its dubious value.
column2: 這太荒謬了。我堂堂一個品德與尊嚴無可置疑的人,竟被逼著把僅剩的一頭索魯斯,以它那本就可疑身價的零頭賤賣出去。

column1: This local reed basket is simple but elegant. You have 1 stored.
column2: 這個當地的蘆葦籃簡樸卻雅致。你存放了 1 個。

column1: This looks like one of the nomadic Brigands who roam the desert in search for travelers to waylay. It looks like he's on his way to his next victim.
column2: 這看來是那些在沙漠遊蕩、伺機攔劫旅人的游牧強盜之一。看樣子他正前往下一個獵物。

column1: This 'painting' shows the city of Shapeir under a starry sky. The entire image has been constructed with dried flowers.
column2: 這幅「畫」描繪著星空下的夏皮爾城。整幅圖都是用乾燥花拼成的。

column1: Throwing the dagger here would be too risky. However, given your elevated position, perhaps you could use it to launch a surprise attack if Khaveen ventures near.
column2: 在這兒擲匕首太冒險。不過,憑你居高臨下的位置,哈維因若敢靠近,你或許能用它發動一記突襲。

column1: To find the Astrologer, you go uppa the street until you can turn right. Then you go downa the street until you can turn right. When you see a door at the end of the street, you finda your place.
column2: 要找占星師,你沿街往上走到能右轉為止,再沿街往下走到能右轉為止。看見街尾那扇門時,你就找著地方了。

column1: To make a dispel potion, I will need the feather of a Griffin, and, let me think... ah, that may be a problem. I need the "Fruit of Compassion."
column2: 要調驅散藥水,我需要一根獅鷲羽毛,還有,讓我想想……啊,那可能有點麻煩。我需要「慈悲之果」。

column1: Unfortunately, the Brigands anticipated that the clearing of the snow would attract traders and they ambushed Abdulla's caravan and stole all of his belongings just before he reached Spielburg Valley.
column2: 可惜,強盜們料到積雪一清會引來商人,於是趁阿布杜拉快抵達史畢柏格谷地時伏擊了他的商隊,搶光了他所有的財物。

column1: Unfortunately, you push it in too far, causing yourself a cerebral hemorrhage. Guess you should have practiced some more on less difficult locks.
column2: 可惜,你伸得太深,害自己腦溢血了。看來你該先拿簡單些的鎖多練練。

column1: Until your job here is done, it would be wise to keep it with you. Your vital organs may end up thanking you for any fatal injuries they DIDN'T suffer.
column2: 在你這兒的差事辦完之前,把它帶著才是明智的。你的要害器官,搞不好會為它們「沒」受的那些致命傷而感激你。

column1: Using both hands, you touch your left knee, your right instep, both temples, and the small spot in between your shoulder blades that always itches.
column2: 你用雙手,碰了碰左膝、右腳背、兩邊太陽穴,還有兩塊肩胛骨之間那塊老是發癢的小地方。

column1: Various kinds of oils and ointments are sold at this stall. Among them are substances such as aloe vera-based sunscreen and strawberry hedgehog moisturizer.
column2: 這攤子賣著各式各樣的油與藥膏,其中包括蘆薈防曬乳,還有草莓刺蝟潤膚乳之類的東西。

column1: Wait a minute! What you think I am, a moneychanger? Your money is no good. You need to get this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buy anything.
column2: 等一下!你當我是什麼,錢莊嗎?你的錢不能用。你得先把它換成第納爾和生丁,才買得了東西。

column1: Wait a minute! What you think I am, a moneychanger? Your money, itsa no good. You need to getta this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buya something.
column2: 等一下!你當我是什麼,錢莊嗎?你那錢啊,不能用。你得先把它換成第納爾和生丁,才買得了東西。

column1: WARNING: Please run the setup program and check the "Use digital music pack" option before playing. This file must be available in order for the game to run.[[Individual music and speech settings may be adjusted, enabled, or disabled from within the game.
column2: 警告:遊玩前請執行設定程式,並勾選「使用數位音樂包」選項。此檔案必須存在,遊戲才能運行。[[個別的音樂與語音設定,可於遊戲內調整、啟用或停用。

column1: Was that 'No, I want to buy a Saurus' or 'I know I want to buy a Saurus?' You've really got to pronounce things clearly, you know. Or maybe you don't.
column2: 你剛說的是「不,我想買頭索魯斯」,還是「我知道我想買頭索魯斯」?你說話真得吐字清楚點,知道吧。也許你不知道。

column1: Watch it buddy! I'm not getting hazardous duty pay on this gig, so don't mess with me. They don't pay me enough to put up with a lowlife like you. Cheeeeeezzzz!
column2: 放尊重點,老兄!我幹這活兒可沒拿危險津貼,別惹我。他們給我的錢,還不夠我忍受你這種下三濫。嘖————!

column1: We first headed to the citadel of Petalumeir to the north of here. From there, we took another caravan following the coast of the Med Sea and then headed north towards the kingdom of Sangerhafen.
column2: 我們先往這兒以北的佩塔露梅爾城而去。從那兒,我們又搭上一支商隊,沿著梅德海岸前行,再往北朝桑格哈芬王國而去。

column1: We had heard that the Baron never left his castle, so Spielburg was very lucky to have the sheriff there to represent the town and maintain order.
column2: 我們聽說那位男爵從不離開城堡,所以史畢柏格能有那位郡長坐鎮、代表全鎮並維持秩序,實在幸運。

column1: We heard about it. Elsa was the one who rallied up the baronial guards and stormed the Brigand fortress in order to prevent the Brigands from escaping with the stolen treasure.
column2: 這事我們聽說了。是艾莎集結男爵的衛隊、強攻強盜堡壘,才攔下強盜帶著贓寶逃走。

column1: We kept asking ourselves how far we still had to travel. We eventually found the answer in a small Adventurer's Guild several days traveling to the west of Wien, the capital of Sangerhafen.
column2: 我們不斷自問:還得走多遠?最終,我們在桑格哈芬首都維恩以西數日路程的一座小冒險者公會裡,找到了答案。

column1: We knew that setting up an inn in Spielburg would mean we would no longer have the funds to continue our journey, but at the same time, we could not afford to spend the winter anywhere without a way to make a living.
column2: 我們知道在史畢柏格開客棧,就意味著再沒錢繼續旅程;但同時,沒有營生,我們也無力在任何地方過冬。

column1: Well, it is obvious you think being a hero is more important than being a scholar. Perhaps someday when you have matured a bit, you will comprehend the enormity of the education you have ignored!
column2: 好吧,你顯然認為當英雄比當學者重要。也許有朝一日你成熟了些,就會明白自己所忽視的那份學問有多麼了不起!

column1: Well, we certainly are going nowhere fast. I'm willing to make you my best offer. Twenty dinars and you ride away on a new (for you) Saurus. What do you say?
column2: 好吧,咱們這樣耗下去顯然沒個結果。我給你我最好的價:二十第納爾,你就能騎著一頭(對你而言)全新的索魯斯走人。怎麼樣?

column1: We met him at your ceremony in Spielburg, but he did not seem to be a very sympathetic person. When we greeted him, he barely acknowledged our presence.
column2: 我們在史畢柏格為你舉行的典禮上見過他,但他似乎不是個討喜的人。我們向他問好時,他幾乎沒搭理我們。

column1: The shutters on this window are tightly closed as if the residence itself wishes to shut its eyes to the current state of the city.
column2: 這扇窗的百葉緊閉,彷彿這宅子自己想對城市的現狀閉上雙眼。

column1: Infiltrating the Brigand fortress and striking at the heart of their threat was not something you could have accomplished by pure luck or brute strength alone. What you displayed more than anything was courage and resourcefulness. We knew that we had finally found the hero that we had been searching for.
column2: 潛入強盜堡壘、直搗其威脅的核心,絕非單憑運氣或蠻力就能辦到的。你所展現的,首要的是勇氣與機智。我們知道,我們終於找到了一直在尋覓的那位英雄。

column1: It would have been one thing if our letter had not reached its destination or if our kin decided not to go out of their way to help us, but for the necessities required for our departure to make it all the way from Shapeir to Spielburg's mountain pass and then be stolen mere miles away from our doorstep was too much.
column2: 信若沒能送達,或族人決意不肯費心相助,那是一回事;可那些供我們啟程所需的物資,千里迢迢從夏皮爾運到史畢柏格的山隘,竟在離我們家門僅幾哩處被搶——這實在太教人難受了。

column1: Over the course of the winter, we had ample time to learn about Spielburg's history. We learned about the Baron becoming a recluse after the loss of his children. We heard about the Brigand band conducting raids. And we heard about the effect of the Brigand menace on the local trade routes and tourist business.
column2: 整個冬天,我們有充裕的時間了解史畢柏格的歷史。我們得知男爵在喪失子女後變得離群索居,聽說了強盜團四處劫掠,也聽聞強盜之患對本地商路與觀光生意的影響。

column1: The robbery was as much a tragedy to us as it was to Abdulla. There had been times during our journey when I came to question the feasibility of our mission. Shema and I were not used to a life on the road and as months went by, we started wondering how much longer we were meant to roam the far reaches of our world.
column2: 那場劫案對我們而言,與對阿布杜拉一樣是場悲劇。旅途中曾有幾度,我開始懷疑我們這趟使命是否可行。雪瑪和我不慣風餐露宿的日子,月復一月,我們漸漸納悶:這天涯海角,我們究竟還註定要漂泊多久。

column1: We spent most of our time there in the confines of our inn, as there were very few places to go in such a small town. And due to the monsters and Brigands in the forest, venturing outside the town gates was too dangerous.
column2: 我們在那兒,多半時間都關在自家客棧裡,畢竟那麼個小鎮也沒幾處可去。再加上森林裡的怪物與強盜,踏出鎮門實在太危險。

column1: We were afraid to embrace hope once again as the previous disappointment still lingered in our minds. But from the outset, we could tell that you were most kind a person and that impressed us greatly.
column2: 先前的失望仍縈繞心頭,我們不敢再次擁抱希望。但打從一開始,我們就看得出你是個極其善良的人,這讓我們深受觸動。

column1: We were not sure how long we could afford to provide him with a free room and board, but we felt partly responsible for his plight and could relate closely to his grief.
column2: 我們不確定還能免費供他食宿多久,但我們覺得自己對他的處境也有一份責任,也對他的悲傷感同身受。

column1: We were told that the very caravan we were on would be the last that traveled west for many months. The Dragonsmoke mountain range near Spielburg is notorious for being difficult to traverse and caravans avoid it completely during the winter months.
column2: 有人告訴我們,我們搭的這支商隊,將是好幾個月內最後一支西行的。史畢柏格附近的龍煙山脈以難以穿越著稱,商隊在冬季月份一概避之。

column1: What I believe made you a hero in the end were not the books you read about adventuring, but your potential and knowledge about your own abilities.
column2: 我相信,最終讓你成為英雄的,並非你讀過的那些冒險書籍,而是你的潛力,以及你對自身能力的了解。

column1: When Abdulla entered our inn with the news that the Brigands had not only taken his belongings but our own wealth as well, I felt like things were collapsing around me.
column2: 當阿布杜拉走進我們客棧,捎來強盜不僅奪走他的財物、連我們的財富也一併搶去的消息時,我只覺得周遭一切都在崩塌。

column1: When mounting an offense, a plunge can be used to follow up a swing for additional damage.
column2: 進攻時,可以接著一記突刺,追加傷害。

column1: When our caravan made a stop in Spielburg, we visited the Adventurer's Guild there and spoke briefly with the Guild Master. We had hoped that a worthy adventurer had indeed claimed the title of hero there and that the Guild Master could inform us of his whereabouts.
column2: 我們的商隊在史畢柏格停留時,我們去了當地的冒險者公會,和公會會長簡短談了談。我們本指望,當地真有一位夠格的冒險者掙得了英雄之名,而公會會長能告訴我們他的下落。

column1: When Shameen first spoke of heading north, I did not know how to react. I have always been much more of a homemaker than an adventurer. The thought of leaving our home behind, not knowing where we were going or when we would return frightened me.
column2: 沙明頭一回提起要北上時,我不知該作何反應。我向來更像個持家的人,而非冒險者。一想到要拋下家園,不知去向何方、也不知何時能返,我便害怕。

column1: When the arcade difficulty level is set to easy or normal, the copilot feature can assist you with certain combat actions. This panel allows you to customize the copilot's priorities. Keep in mind that the copilot only assists you in battle. It will not fight the entire battle for you.
column2: 當街機難度設為簡單或普通時,戰鬥輔助功能能在某些戰鬥動作上協助你。此面板讓你自訂輔助的優先順序。請記住,輔助只是在戰鬥中協助你,並不會替你打完整場仗。

column1: When the fate of our kinsmen reached the Sultan, he immediately sent his army to set this injustice right. Unfortunately, his men never reached the gates of Raseir.
column2: 我族人的遭遇傳到蘇丹耳中,他立即派軍要糾正這樁不義。可惜,他的人馬從未抵達拉希爾的城門。

column1: Where was I? Oh yes. You tried to pass me counterfeit coins. I counter with a fit. We agreed upon dinars, not whatever you tried to give me. You need to see the Moneychanger before we can deal again.
column2: 我說到哪了?喔對。你想拿假錢幣糊弄我。我可要跟你翻臉。咱們講好的是第納爾,不是你想塞給我的那玩意兒。你得先去找錢莊,我們才能再做買賣。

column1: While Abdulla may seem somewhat foolish at times, he understands the value of friendship very well. He promised us that if he ever recovered his flying carpet from the Brigands, he would take us anywhere we wished to go, even if that meant leaving behind any of his treasure that would not fit on the carpet.
column2: 阿布杜拉有時雖顯得有些憨,卻很懂友情的可貴。他答應我們,只要哪天從強盜手中奪回他的飛毯,就帶我們去任何想去的地方,哪怕得撇下毯子載不下的財寶也在所不惜。

column1: While Raseir and Shapeir are supposed to be twin cities, a large part of the city appears to be blocked off. At least you can use the map to see where you are.
column2: 拉希爾與夏皮爾照理說是雙子城,但這城裡有一大片地方似乎被封了起來。至少你還能用地圖看清自己身在何處。

column1: While you have no idea what a bumper is, you instinctively know that this is a bumper sticker. It says: "Back off man, I'm a professional hero."
column2: 你雖然不知道「保險桿」是什麼,卻本能地知道這是張保險桿貼紙。上頭寫著:「閃邊去,老兄,我可是專業英雄。」

column1: With fiery emotion, you explain to Harik that the Earth Elemental is attacking the city. At first, he seems unconvinced, but your eloquence finally sways him.
column2: 你情緒激昂地向哈里克說明土元素精靈正在攻擊這座城市。起初他似乎半信半疑,但你的口才終究說服了他。

column1: With the roads to and from the valley cut off by the snow, our inn understandably had very little business. We were only able to make a living thanks to Shema's most excellent cooking skills.
column2: 進出谷地的道路為大雪所阻,我們客棧的生意自然清淡得很。全靠雪瑪一手出色的廚藝,我們才得以維生。

column1: You already took a feather, so don't be greedy. The Griffin probably needs them more than you.
column2: 你已經拿走一根羽毛了,別貪心。那獅鷲八成比你更需要它們。

column1: You ask Dinarzad how much she would pay for a key that would grant one access to the Katta's Tail Inn without arousing suspicion from patrolling guards.
column2: 你問迪娜扎德,一把能讓人進出「卡塔之尾客棧」、又不致引起巡邏衛兵起疑的鑰匙,她願意出多少錢。

column1: This is not a good place to practice your throwing.
column2: 這裡不是練投擲的好地方。

column1: INVENTORY: The bottom of the inventory screen also shows the maximum weight your hero can carry to the left and the current weight your hero is carrying to the right. If you exceed the maximum allowed weight your hero will become overloaded and will begin to move slowly and more awkwardly. If this happens you should drop some items until you are no longer overloaded.
column2: 物品欄:物品欄畫面底部,左邊顯示你英雄能負荷的最大重量,右邊顯示他目前所背的重量。一旦超出允許的最大重量,你的英雄就會超重,行動變得遲緩而笨拙。若發生這種情況,你該丟下一些物品,直到不再超重為止。

column1: SPECIAL ACTIONS MENU[[WALK: Change to walking mode.[[RUN: Change to running mode.[[SNEAK: Change to sneaking mode (only if you have Stealth skill).[[REST: Allows you to recover some health and stamina or pass time.[[STATISTICS: Shows your Hero's skill statistics sheet.[[TIME OF DAY: Shows the current time and day in Shapeir.[[OK: Closes the Special Actions menu.
column2: 特殊動作選單[[行走:切換為行走模式。[[奔跑:切換為奔跑模式。[[潛行:切換為潛行模式(僅在你具備潛行技能時)。[[休息:讓你恢復部分生命力與耐力,或讓時間流逝。[[屬性:顯示你英雄的技能屬性表。[[時辰:顯示夏皮爾當前的時辰與日期。[[確定:關閉特殊動作選單。

column1: THE OPTIONS MENU[[GAME SKILL: Alters the difficulty of combat and other puzzles. Increase the slider to increase the challenge.[[SOUND VOLUME: Alters the volume of in-game sound effects. Drag the slider higher to increase the volume or lower to decrease it.[[MUSIC VOLUME: As above, but for music.[[GAME SPEED: Alters the game's speed according to the slider level.
column2: 選項選單[[遊戲難度:調整戰鬥與其他謎題的難度。將滑桿往上調可提高挑戰性。[[音效音量:調整遊戲內音效的音量。滑桿往高處拉可增大音量,往低處拉可減小。[[音樂音量:同上,但用於音樂。[[遊戲速度:依滑桿的高低調整遊戲速度。

column1: THE OPTIONS MENU[[GAME SKILL: Alters the difficulty of combat. Increase the slider to increase the challenge.[[SOUND VOLUME: Alters the volume of in-game sound effects. Drag the slider higher to increase the volume or lower to decrease it.[[MUSIC VOLUME: As above, but for music.[[GAME SPEED: Alters the game's speed according to the slider level.
column2: 選項選單[[遊戲難度:調整戰鬥的難度。將滑桿往上調可提高挑戰性。[[音效音量:調整遊戲內音效的音量。滑桿往高處拉可增大音量,往低處拉可減小。[[音樂音量:同上,但用於音樂。[[遊戲速度:依滑桿的高低調整遊戲速度。

column1: Well, you go north until you get to the fountain and from the fountain's north point, you turn-a to the left.
column2: 這樣啊,你往北走到噴泉,再從噴泉的北端往左拐。

column1: Well, you go north until you see a way to go left, and you take-a the way. Now you go until you see a way to go right, and you take-a thatta way.
column2: 這樣啊,你往北走,看到能往左的路就走那條。接著一直走,看到能往右的路,就走那條。

column1: You can make out the mountains beyond the buildings to the south.
column2: 你能辨認出南面建築之外的群山。

column1: You can't do that while riding your Saurus. You'd fall off!
column2: 你騎著索魯斯時沒法那麼做,會摔下來的!

column1: You carefully hide your thief license inside. That's one piece of incriminating evidence off your body. Now all you have to do is make sure you aren't caught red-handed.
column2: 你小心地把盜賊執照藏了進去。這下身上少了一件罪證。現在你只消確保自己別當場被逮個正著就成。

column1: You detect an intense aura of dark magic emanating from the Ghoul. Its very presence seems to drain the strength right out of your Detect Magic spell.
column2: 你偵測到食屍鬼(Ghoul)散發出一股濃烈的黑暗魔法氣息。光是它在場,似乎就把你「偵測魔法」術的力道吸得一乾二淨。

column1: You don't work for the Shapeirian Sanitation Department. Besides, do you really want to carry around every piece of trash you find laying in the street?
column2: 你又不是夏皮爾清潔隊的。再說,你真想把街上撿到的每一片垃圾都帶在身上嗎?

column1: You drop one of your rocks. An idea flashes through your mind. Marking your trail with small rocks may be a very useful way to avoid getting lost in the desert.
column2: 你掉了一塊石頭。一個念頭閃過你腦海:用小石頭標記路徑,或許是在沙漠裡避免迷路的好法子。

column1: You eat a quarter of the doughy disk. Its flavor is unlike anything you've ever tasted. You feel invigorated, revitalized, and ready to take on the world.
column2: 你吃了那塊麵餅的四分之一。那滋味是你從未嘗過的。你覺得精力充沛、煥然一新,準備好征服世界。

column1: You eat the last quarter of the doughy disk. Its flavor is as magnificent as always, but you feel a sense of regret knowing that it's the last slice you'll ever get to eat.
column2: 你吃下那塊麵餅的最後四分之一。滋味一如既往地絕妙,但一想到這是你此生能吃到的最後一塊,你不禁生出一絲遺憾。

column1: You faintly hear "To the left... up... ah, right there!" The lamp glows faintly with pleasure, then decides it isn't dark enough to really put on a show, and goes out again.
column2: 你隱約聽見:「往左……往上……啊,就是那兒!」那盞燈愜意地微微一亮,旋即覺得這兒還不夠暗,不值得它好好表演一番,便又熄了。

column1: You go until you can see a way to go right and you go to the right. Then you go until you can go to the right again. Thatsa right way.
column2: 你一直走,看到能往右的路就往右。接著再走,直到又有個能往右的地方。沒錯,就那條路。

column1: You have captured your first screenshot. You may capture 99 more. If you quit this program, restart it, and take new screenshots, existing screenshots will be overwritten.
column2: 你已截取第一張螢幕擷圖,還可再截 99 張。你若離開本程式、重啟後再截新圖,現有的擷圖將被覆蓋。

column1: You have in your possession a beautifully crafted dagger made of pure silver which was once the property of Jabbar bin Ma'amar.
column2: 你擁有一把以純銀打造、做工精美的匕首,它曾是賈巴爾·賓·馬阿瑪爾的財產。

column1: You can't do that while riding your Saurus.
column2: 你騎著索魯斯時沒法那麼做。

column1: You have 1 empty clay pot.
column2: 你有 1 個空陶罐。

column1: You have no desire to announce your presence to the patrolling eunuch.
column2: 你無意向那名巡邏的宦官表明自己在場。

column1: You have no idea who that guy is, but he certainly looks out of place in a town in the middle of the desert... (well, perhaps not THAT out of place).
column2: 你不知道那傢伙是誰,但他在這沙漠正中的小鎮裡,看著實在格格不入……(嗯,或許也沒「那麼」格格不入)。

column1: You have only to give me a dinar, and if you manage to cross the rope from one end to the other, then you will gain ten dinars more.
column2: 你只要給我一第納爾,要是能從繩子這頭走到那頭,就能再贏得十第納爾。

column1: You have selected the classic alleyway system. This option will allow Quest for Glory veterans to explore the city as it was originally designed.
column2: 你選了經典巷弄系統。此選項讓英雄傳奇的老手能照原始設計探索這座城市。

column1: You have selected the simplified alleyway system. This option will allow players new to Quest for Glory II to navigate the city with little chance of getting lost.
column2: 你選了簡化巷弄系統。此選項讓初次接觸英雄傳奇 II 的玩家,在城裡走動時幾乎不會迷路。

column1: You have selected the Text Parser interface. You'll be able to directly access each conversation topic without having to look it up in dialogue trees. A good option for Quest for Glory veterans.
column2: 你選了文字指令介面。你將能直接觸及每個談話主題,不必在對話樹中翻找。對英雄傳奇老手是個不錯的選擇。

column1: You never did return to Shapeir again, because, for some reason the WIT Wizards were unable to explain, the door to that land became permanently closed. They believe it had something to do with the land being destroyed.
column2: 你再也沒能回到夏皮爾,因為基於某種連巫師技術學院的巫師也解釋不了的緣故,通往那片土地的門永遠關閉了。他們相信,這與那片土地的毀滅有關。

column1: You place your thumb upon your nose with the hand held perpendicular to the face and the fingers outspread. You then wiggle your fingers while focusing your eyes on the thumb and patting your belly with the other hand.
column2: 你把拇指抵在鼻尖,手與臉垂直、五指張開,接著一邊扭動手指、目光盯著拇指,一邊用另一隻手拍著肚子。

column1: You put the map inside the chest. Nothing beats the experience of getting through a maze of alleyways using nothing but your intuition, after all.
column2: 你把地圖放進箱子。畢竟,單憑直覺穿越迷宮般的巷弄,那種體驗無可比擬。

column1: Your character has been imported as a magic user. If you do not feel this is the correct profession, please indicate your preference below...
column2: 你的角色已匯入為法師。你若覺得這職業不對,請於下方指明你的偏好……

column1: You realize that you don't have 50 centimes to pay the stable and board fee. You tell the guard you'll pay him as soon as you get some loose change.
